findings.title: Offline-logged food is silently lost when reconnecting: server state unconditionally overwrites richer local day
findings.evidence: syncHistoryFromServer: `final daily = _dailyFromServerJson(raw); await _saveDayToPrefs(dayDate, daily);` (no merge); getDay L799-804: `if (remoteEmpty && localHas) { daily = local; ... } else { await _saveDayToPrefs(date, daily); }`; push failure swallowed at L658-660: `catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'nutrition:push-day'); }` with no retry queue
findings.detail: Scenario: user logs breakfast online (synced), later logs lunch offline — addEntry saves locally but _pushDayToServer fails silently (reportError only, never retried). On next tab open, _load() runs syncHistoryFromServer(days:14) which writes the server's stale copy of today (breakfast only) straight over local prefs, and getDay's merge guard only protects when the remote day is COMPLETELY empty (entries empty AND water 0 AND weight null). The offline lunch entry is permanently deleted. This is real data loss and directly violates the offline-first principle (workout/food tracking must work offline and sync on reconnect).
findings.fix: Queue failed _pushDayToServer payloads (dirty-day flag per date) and replay before syncing down; in getDay/syncHistoryFromServer, never overwrite a locally-dirty day with server data — merge by entry id or last-write-wins per entry instead of whole-day replacement.

findings.title: XP claim card is a dead feature presented as live — tapping 'Claim today's XP' shows fabricated '+0 XP / Already claimed'
findings.evidence: `Future<NutritionXpClaimResult> claimDayXp() async { return const NutritionXpClaimResult(xpAwarded: 0, totalXp: 0, lines: [], alreadyClaimed: true); }` — comment admits: 'Stubbed during the merge — the backend nutrition-xp service was removed... The feature is currently inert'. Card is still mounted at nutrition_tab.dart:412 and promises (nutrition_xp_claim_card.dart:126): 'Hit your daily macros (within ±10%) to earn XP.'
findings.detail: The card renders a full pre-claim pitch and an enabled 'Claim today's XP' button. Tapping it always renders the post-claim state '+0 XP' with the 'Already claimed' label — a lie on two counts: the user never claimed, and no XP system is wired. This is exactly the fabricated-data pattern the owner forbids, and it burns trust in the gamification loop.
findings.fix: Remove/hide NutritionXpClaimCard from nutrition_tab.dart until the backend endpoint returns, or replace with an honest 'XP rewards coming back soon' disabled state.

findings.title: Notification bell dot is hardcoded on — always claims unread notifications
findings.evidence: `_NutritionHeaderBtn(icon: Icons.notifications_none_rounded, hasDot: true, onTap: ...NotificationsScreen()...)` — hasDot is a literal true, never derived from notification state
findings.detail: The brand-colored badge dot signals 'you have notifications' permanently, regardless of whether any exist. Fabricated state indicator; users learn to ignore the badge.
findings.fix: Drive hasDot from the notification service's unread count (or remove the dot until that wiring exists).

findings.title: Nutrition tab date frozen at first build — food logged after midnight lands on the previous day
findings.evidence: `final DateTime _today = DateTime.now();` — a final field initialized once at State creation; used for getDay(_today), removeEntry(id, _today), updateWater/_today, and _WeeklyPlanCard's isToday highlight
findings.detail: Main-nav tabs typically live in an IndexedStack and keep their State for the whole app session. If the app stays open across midnight, every add/remove/water/weight write goes to yesterday's day key, and the weekly-plan 'today' highlight is wrong. MealEntry.loggedAt uses DateTime.now() so addEntry writes to the correct new day, but removeEntry/updateWater/updateWeight and the rendered day all use the stale _today — the tab shows yesterday's totals as 'today'.
findings.fix: Compute today inside _load()/build (e.g., `DateTime get _today => DateTime.now();` normalized to date-only) instead of caching it in a final field.

findings.title: Barcode lookup conflates network/server errors with 'Product not found in database'
findings.evidence: searchByBarcode: `} catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'nutrition:lookup-gtin'); return null; }` and nutrition_tab.dart:1724-1726 maps every null to `SnackBar(content: Text('Product not found in database.'))`
findings.detail: Offline or with a 429/503 from USDA, the user scans a valid product and is told it doesn't exist in the database — a false statement that discourages retrying. Note the name-search path does this correctly (FoodNameSearchResult carries errorMessage per failure class); barcode lookup throws that information away.
findings.fix: Return a result type (found / notFound / error+message) from searchByBarcode, mirroring searchByName, and show 'Couldn't reach the food database — check your connection' for the error case.

findings.title: Swipe-to-delete meal entry does a server round trip before removing the row — Dismissible can throw and rapid swipes can resurrect entries
findings.evidence: `onRemove: (id) async { await _service.removeEntry(id, _today); await _load(); }` where removeEntry (nutrition_service.dart:845-857) first calls getDay() — a network GET — before filtering and pushing; the dismissed row stays in `_day.entries` until _load() completes
findings.detail: Two problems: (1) the dismissed Dismissible remains in the tree for the duration of a network round trip (getDay fetch + PUT + full _load incl. 14-day syncHistoryFromServer); any rebuild in that window triggers Flutter's 'A dismissed Dismissible widget is still part of the tree' exception. (2) swiping two entries quickly runs two removeEntry calls concurrently — the second's getDay can fetch server state that still contains the first entry, re-saving it locally (deleted item reappears).
findings.fix: Remove the entry from _day.entries synchronously in onDismissed (setState), then persist/push in the background; have removeEntry operate on the local prefs copy instead of re-fetching from the server first.

findings.title: Bottom 'Add food' CTA silently logs everything to Breakfast — the search sheet never shows or lets you change the target meal
findings.evidence: `onPressed: () => _showAddFood('breakfast'),` on the primary full-width 'Add food' button; _AddFoodSheet and _PortionSheet render food name, brand, portion and macros but never display `widget.meal`
findings.detail: A user tapping the big CTA at 8pm gets their dinner logged under Breakfast with zero indication. There is no meal label anywhere in the add flow and no way to change it after selection — they'd have to notice the misplaced entry, swipe-delete it, and re-add from the Dinner section header.
findings.fix: Default the meal by time of day (e.g., <11h breakfast, <16h lunch, <21h dinner, else snack) and show a meal selector chip row in _AddFoodSheet/_PortionSheet.

findings.title: Journal delete swallows DB errors — UI shows 'Entry deleted' and pops even when the delete failed
findings.evidence: `Future<void> deleteEntry(int id) async { try { ... await db.delete(...); } catch (e) { debugPrint('[journal-store] deleteEntry failed: $e'); } }` — never rethrows; journal_entry_screen.dart:156-164 then unconditionally shows `SnackBar(content: Text('Entry deleted'))` and pops with deletedSentinel
findings.detail: The composer's try/catch around deleteEntry (journal_entry_screen.dart:155-171) is dead code: the store catches internally and returns normally, so the 'Could not delete' branch can never run. On failure the user is told the entry is deleted, the screen pops, the list reloads, and the entry is still there.
findings.fix: Let deleteEntry rethrow (the screen already handles the error path), keeping reportError for telemetry.

findings.title: Daily goals sheet accepts 0 (NaN progress) and silently replaces blanks with hardcoded defaults
findings.evidence: `calories: int.tryParse(_calCtrl.text) ?? 2000,` (blank → silently 2000; '0' → 0) and _CaloriesCard L893: `final progress = (consumed / goal).clamp(0.0, 1.0);` with no goal>0 guard (cf. _MacroCard L1003 which does guard `goal > 0 ? ... : 0.0`)
findings.detail: Saving a calories goal of 0 makes consumed/goal NaN (0/0) or Infinity; the progress bar renders garbage/nothing and the chip shows '0 left'. Clearing a field and saving silently writes 2000/150/65/250/2500 — values the user never typed — to both prefs and the server profile (PATCH /me/profile).
findings.fix: Validate inputs (min 1, sane ranges), disable Save when invalid, and guard _CaloriesCard's division like _MacroCard already does.

findings.title: Goals bottom sheet has no scroll view — overflow risk with keyboard open on small screens
findings.evidence: `padding: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom), child: Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, ...` containing 5 TextFields + title + 52px button with no SingleChildScrollView
findings.detail: Fixed content (~470dp) plus keyboard inset (~300dp) exceeds available height on shorter devices, producing a RenderFlex overflow and making the lower fields (Water) and the Save button unreachable while the keyboard is up.
findings.fix: Wrap the sheet body in SingleChildScrollView (keep viewInsets bottom padding).

findings.title: Regenerating the weekly AI plan replaces the entire Nutrition tab with a bare spinner for 30-120s
findings.evidence: `// Same UX guard as _bootstrapWeekPlan — show the spinner so the user doesn't tap repeatedly while DeepSeek is grinding for ~60-120s. if (mounted) setState(() => _loading = true);` — _loading=true swaps the whole body (L371-372) for a Center(CircularProgressIndicator)
findings.detail: While the AI plan generates (the code's own comment says 30-120 seconds on a cold dyno), the user cannot log food, water, weight, or view today's macros — the entire tab is a contextless spinner with no progress text and no cancel. The same happens on the auto-bootstrap right after onboarding, which is the very first impression of the tab.
findings.fix: Scope the busy state to the weekly-plan card (inline spinner + 'Generating your plan, ~1-2 min…' text) and leave the rest of the tab interactive.

findings.title: Food name search excludes Branded foods — packaged products only reachable via barcode
findings.evidence: searchFoods payload: `'dataType': ['Foundation', 'SR Legacy', 'Survey (FNDDS)'],` while lookupBrandedByGtin uses `'dataType': ['Branded']`
findings.detail: Typing a product name like 'oreo' or a protein bar brand returns 'No results' even though USDA has the Branded record — the user must physically have the package and scan its barcode. No hint in the empty state explains this limitation (the helper text only says 'USDA FoodData Central · type 3+ letters').
findings.fix: Include 'Branded' in the name-search dataType list (rank generic foods first if noise is a concern), or state in the 'No results' empty state that packaged foods need a barcode scan.

findings.title: Sub-44pt touch targets and missing semantics in nutrition header and portion controls
findings.evidence: _NutritionHeaderBtn: `Container(width: 40, height: 40, ...)` inside a bare GestureDetector (no Semantics label/tooltip); 'Edit goals' (L425-435) is a 12px GestureDetector Text; _PortionToggleChip/_PortionQuickChip (L2043-2089) are GestureDetector containers ~31-37px tall
findings.detail: Multiple interactive elements fall below the 44×44pt minimum from the project's non-negotiable accessibility principle, and the GestureDetector-based ones expose no button semantics or labels to screen readers (the bell and tune icons are unlabeled).
findings.fix: Wrap small taps in a 44pt minimum hit area (IconButton with constraints, or Semantics(button:true,label:...) + padded GestureDetector); give 'Edit goals' a padded tappable area.

findings.title: Error UI colored with ZveltTokens.cardio instead of the error token
findings.evidence: _showPersistentError: `SnackBar(backgroundColor: ZveltTokens.cardio, ...)`; the search error banner (L1533) and entry-delete background (L1312) also use `ZveltTokens.cardio.withValues(alpha: ...)`
findings.detail: Nutrition's error/destructive surfaces use the cardio activity color while the journal area in the same audit scope correctly uses ZveltTokens.error (journal_entry_screen.dart:147,453). Semantic token misuse — if the cardio accent ever changes hue, error states change with it.
findings.fix: Use ZveltTokens.error for error snackbars, error banners, and the delete swipe background.

findings.title: Journal history hard-capped at 30 entries with no way to view older ones
findings.evidence: `static const int _kRecentLimit = 30;` → `JournalStore.instance.getEntries(limit: _kRecentLimit)`; no pagination, 'view all', or date navigation anywhere in the tab
findings.detail: After a month of daily journaling, entries older than the newest 30 are stored in SQLite but unreachable from any UI — a silent dead end for exactly the 'patterns over time' value the empty state promises.
findings.fix: Add lazy pagination (load-more on scroll) or a month picker that queries rangeMap for older windows.

findings.title: Joined state never hydrated from server — already-joined races show JOIN and re-tap inflates the athlete count
findings.evidence: final Map<String, bool> _joinedChallenges = {}; ... else { await _challengeService.joinChallenge(challengeId); ... _participantCounts[challengeId] = (_participantCounts[challengeId] ?? 0) + 1; }
findings.detail: _joinedChallenges starts empty and is never populated from the backend (getChallengeParticipants returns participant data but only 'total' is consumed at line 257). A user who already joined a race (e.g. via the feed hero card, or in a previous session) always sees 'JOIN THE RACE'. Tapping it hits POST /join which returns 200 'already joined' (treated as success in social_challenge_service.dart:192) and the local count is incremented anyway — the card now displays a participant count one higher than reality (fabricated data). The joined/left state is also lost every time the screen is reopened.
findings.fix: Hydrate _joinedChallenges from the participants response (check if current userId is in result['data']) inside _loadParticipantCounts, and re-fetch the count after join/leave instead of incrementing optimistically without ground truth.

findings.title: Race Notes promise 'PRIVATE · ONLY YOU' but every note is POSTed to a server chat endpoint
findings.evidence: unawaited(_postToServer(challengeId, trimmed)); ... Uri.parse('$v1Base/challenges/$challengeId/chat/messages')
findings.detail: The UI explicitly promises privacy three times — the header pill 'PRIVATE · ONLY YOU' (race_hub_screen.dart:850), the banner 'Private notepad ... the room is yours' (line 900-908), and the input hint 'Drop a note (only you see this)…' (line 1021). Yet sendMessage fire-and-forgets every note body to POST /v1/challenges/{id}/chat/messages — an endpoint designed to become a multi-athlete chat room in v1.1. The moment the backend ships that endpoint, notes users wrote under an 'only you see this' promise are sitting in a shared chat table (and may already be logged server-side). This directly violates 'privacy by default'.
findings.fix: Remove the _postToServer call for v1.0 (keep local-only persistence), or change the UI copy to disclose that notes are uploaded. When v1.1 chat ships, require fresh opt-in before any historical or new note leaves the device.

findings.title: Circle 'TOP STREAKS' is not sorted by streak — it's just the first 3 friends in list order
findings.evidence: List<_StreakUser> get _topStreaks => _filteredFriends.take(3).map((f) { ... days: resolved ? _friendStreaks[f.userId] : null,
findings.detail: The section is titled '🔥 TOP STREAKS' (line 337) and renders flame badges with day counts, but the three users shown are simply _filteredFriends.take(3) — whatever order /v1/friends returned. A friend with a 50-day streak can be absent while a friend with 0 days is showcased as a 'top streak'. The streak numbers themselves are real, but the 'TOP' claim is fabricated ranking.
findings.fix: After streaks resolve, sort friends by _friendStreaks value descending (nulls last) before take(3); until resolved, either hide the section or label it neutrally.

findings.title: Circle 'RECENTLY ACTIVE' fabricates recency — it's the first 5 friends with no activity data, and friends beyond 5 are invisible
findings.evidence: List<_RecentUser> get _recent => _filteredFriends.take(5).map((f) { return _RecentUser(... sub: 'IN YOUR CIRCLE', ... online: false,
findings.detail: The list under the 'RECENTLY ACTIVE' heading (line 362) is just take(5) of the friends list — no last-active timestamp is fetched. The heading plus the green pulsing-dot badge next to it implies live presence/recency that does not exist. Additionally, a user with more than 5 friends has no way to see the rest on this screen except by typing a search query — there is no 'view all' path other than the separate FriendsScreen.
findings.fix: Rename the section to 'YOUR CIRCLE' (matching the row subtitle that is already honest), drop the green presence-style badge or repurpose it as a plain count chip, and show the full friend list or add a 'See all' link.

findings.title: Race hub shows 'No active races — Be the first to launch one!' while the list is still loading and after load failures
findings.evidence:
if (_trending.isEmpty)
  const ZveltEmptyState(... title: 'No active races', subtitle: 'Be the first to launch one!',)
findings.detail: There is no loading flag for _loadChallenges: _challenges starts empty, the network fetch can take up to 22s (social_challenge_service.dart:51 timeout), and during that whole window the user sees a definitive 'No active races' empty state. If _loadChallenges throws (line 109-111), the error goes only to the crash reporter — same misleading empty state, no retry affordance, and no pull-to-refresh on the ListView (the only refresh path is hidden inside the settings bottom sheet).
findings.fix: Add a _loadingChallenges flag with a spinner/skeleton for the trending row, an inline error state with retry on failure, and wrap the ListView in a RefreshIndicator.

findings.title: Deep-link snackbar claims "You're in" even when the join API call failed
findings.evidence: try { await _challengeService.joinChallenge(c.id); } catch (e, st) { // Already-joined / network failures are non-fatal ... reportError(...); }
findings.detail: _joinAndOpenRace swallows ALL join failures (network error, 500, 'Not signed in') and still opens RaceHubScreen with initialChallengeId, where _maybeOpenDeepLink unconditionally shows the snackbar "You're in: ${matched.title}" (race_hub_screen.dart:160). A user whose join silently failed is told they joined a race they are not in.
findings.fix: Return a success bool from the join attempt and pass it into the hub (or have the hub verify membership via the participants endpoint) so the snackbar only claims membership when it's true; on failure show 'Couldn't join — retry'.

findings.title: JOIN always fails with a 404 on locally-created (offline) races
findings.evidence: id: now.millisecondsSinceEpoch.toString(), ... // joinChallenge: Uri.parse('$v1Base/challenges/$challengeId/join')
findings.detail: When publish() falls back to local storage (no token or network error, social_challenge_service.dart:113,160-163), the challenge keeps its millisecondsSinceEpoch id. loadActive() serves these local rows in the trending list, whose JOIN button calls joinChallenge() — which POSTs to the server unconditionally and will 404 (or 'Not signed in'), surfacing a raw 'Could not join challenge (404)' snackbar. The service even has _looksLikeUuid() for exactly this distinction but only uses it in remove().
findings.fix: Reuse _looksLikeUuid in joinChallenge/leaveChallenge (or in _toggleJoin) and handle local-only races client-side, e.g. mark joined locally or hide the JOIN button for unsynced races.

findings.title: Race creator is not a participant of their own race
findings.evidence: Future<void> _launchRace() async { ... await _challengeService.publish(draft); ... _openRaceChat(fresh);
findings.detail: _launchRace publishes the challenge and immediately opens the notes screen, but never calls joinChallenge for the creator (and there's no evidence the client expects the server to auto-join — _joinedChallenges stays false and the count shows 0). Back on the hub the creator's own race displays 'JOIN THE RACE', which is confusing — they must manually join the race they just built to be counted.
findings.fix: After a successful publish with a server id, call joinChallenge(serverId) (or set _joinedChallenges[id]=true if the backend auto-joins creators) before showing the confirmation.

findings.title: DM push refresh discards loaded history and force-scrolls the reader to the bottom
findings.evidence: setState(() { _msgs = list; // Re-anchor pagination state — a silent reload always replaces the currently-visible window ... }); WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback((_) => _scrollToEnd());
findings.detail: _silentReload (triggered by any FCM bump on RefreshScope.dm) replaces _msgs with only the newest page and then jumps the scroll position to maxScrollExtent. A user who tapped 'Load earlier' several times and is reading old history gets their loaded pages thrown away and is yanked to the newest message whenever any DM push arrives — even one for a different conversation, since the scope is global.
findings.fix: In _silentReload, append only messages newer than _msgs.last.id instead of replacing the window, and only auto-scroll when the user is already at (or near) the bottom.

findings.title: Incoming friend requests cannot be declined
findings.evidence: FilledButton(onPressed: () => _accept(r), child: const Text('Accept')),
findings.detail: The Incoming section of the Requests tab renders only an Accept button per row — there is no Decline/Ignore action, even though the service already exposes removeOrCancel(userId) (friends_service.dart:182) which is used for the outgoing 'Cancel'. An unwanted request sits in the list forever; the only escape is accepting or hunting down the user to block them. For a privacy-by-default product this is a notable gap.
findings.fix: Add a 'Decline' TextButton next to Accept that calls _service.removeOrCancel(r.userId) and reloads.

findings.title: Friend rows and search results don't open the user's profile; long-press on a friend row triggers Block
findings.detail: Tapping a friend tile or a search-result row does nothing — there is no navigation to UserProfileScreen anywhere in friends_screen.dart (the import isn't even present), so from the Friends list you cannot view a friend's profile/posts. Meanwhile the hidden gesture that IS wired (long-press) opens the Block dialog — a destructive action duplicated by the visible block icon button, and a surprising thing to discover by accident. Same gap in circle_screen.dart: rows only expose a chat button.
findings.fix: Make row tap push UserProfileScreen(userId: u.userId, ...) and drop the long-press block binding (the explicit block icon already covers it).

findings.title: Profile stats card shows '0' Sessions/PRs/Streak when the backend simply didn't send stats
findings.evidence: final stats = <(String, int)>[ ('Sessions', s?.sessions ?? 0), ('PRs', s?.prs ?? 0), ('Streak', s?.streak ?? 0), ];
findings.detail: _ProfileSummary keeps sessions/prs/streak nullable precisely to distinguish 'unknown' from 0 (the model comment at line 606-607 even says so), but the card coalesces null to 0. Until the backend includes a stats block, every profile renders '0 SESSIONS · 0 PRS · 0 STREAK' for users who may have hundreds of sessions — fabricated zeros, the exact pattern the owner forbids. Circle_screen got this right with its dim '—' for unknown streaks.
findings.fix: Render '—' (with reduced opacity) when the value is null, or hide the stats card entirely when s.sessions == null && s.prs == null && s.streak == null.

findings.title: Profile posts: any non-2xx response silently renders 'No posts yet'
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode < 200 || res.statusCode >= 300) { setState(() { _posts = []; _loadingPosts = false; }); return; }
findings.detail: _loadPosts treats 403, 500, etc. identically to an empty list: _posts=[] with no error flag, so the body shows the 'No posts yet' empty state (line 390-397). A friend whose posts endpoint momentarily 500s appears to have never posted — misleading, and there's no per-section retry (only full pull-to-refresh). The catch block (line 181-184) behaves the same way for network errors.
findings.fix: Track a _postsError flag and render a compact ZveltErrorState with a retry button under the 'Posts' header instead of the empty state when the request failed.

findings.title: Trending race card: title has no maxLines inside a fixed-height card — long titles overflow
findings.evidence: Text(r.title, style: TextStyle(fontFamily: ZveltTokens.fontPrimary, fontSize: 16, ... height: 1)), // inside SizedBox(height: 148) ... width: 168
findings.detail: The horizontal trending list constrains each card to 168x148 (lines 476, 488) and the inner Column ends with a Spacer + button. The title Text has no maxLines/overflow, so a long server-provided customTitle (caption-length titles are allowed) wraps to 3+ lines and triggers a RenderFlex overflow, clipping the JOIN button. The hub's own configurator only generates short titles, but titles come from the backend feed.
findings.fix: Add maxLines: 2, overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis to the title Text.

findings.title: Touch targets below 44pt in race hub/notes: 28px JOIN button, 30px quick-note chips, 18px emoji icon
findings.evidence: child: AnimatedContainer(duration: const Duration(milliseconds: 200), height: 28, ...) // JOIN THE RACE / LEAVE RACE
findings.detail: The primary join/leave action on each trending card is a 28px-tall GestureDetector (line 534-555). The quick-note chips are height 30 (line 966-968) and the emoji-picker trigger is a bare 18px icon in a GestureDetector (line 1030-1035). All are below the 44pt minimum the project mandates; none have Semantics labels either (plain GestureDetector + Container, not buttons).
findings.fix: Wrap these in at least 44px hit areas (e.g. behavior: HitTestBehavior.opaque with padding, or Material buttons) and add Semantics(button: true, label: ...).

findings.title: 'EXPLORE ALL' in the race hub looks like a link but is dead text
findings.evidence: Text('EXPLORE ALL', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 9, fontWeight: FontWeight.w900, color: ZveltTokens.brand, letterSpacing: 1.5)),
findings.detail: Next to the GLOBAL TRENDING header, 'EXPLORE ALL' is styled in brand color and CTA caps — visually identical to a tappable link — but it's a bare Text with no gesture handler and no destination. This is the same stub-button class the recent 'fix dead/stub buttons' commit (317f023) targeted, and it slipped through.
findings.fix: Either remove it (the horizontal list already shows all loaded races) or wire it to a full-list screen.

findings.title: 'You're in' header chat button opens the notes of an arbitrary race (_challenges.first)
findings.evidence: onTap: _challenges.isEmpty ? null : () => _openRaceChat(_challenges.first),
findings.detail: The gradient chat button in the hub header always opens Race Notes for whichever challenge happens to be first in the feed — not a race the user joined or created. The user has no indication which race's notepad they landed in beyond the small subtitle.
findings.fix: Prefer the first race the user has joined (once joined state is hydrated), or show a race picker when multiple races exist.

findings.title: RaceChatService.clearForChallenge is never called — its 'called when the user leaves a race' contract is false
findings.evidence:
/// Wipe history for a single race — called when the user leaves a race.
Future<void> clearForChallenge(String challengeId) async {
findings.detail: Grep shows zero call sites outside the service. _toggleJoin's leave branch (race_hub_screen.dart:271-276) does not clear notes, so the doc comment misdescribes actual behavior. Notes for left/expired races accumulate in the encrypted DB indefinitely. Not user-visible today, but the stale contract will mislead whoever wires v1.1 chat.
findings.fix: Either call clearForChallenge from the leave flow (possibly after a confirm, since notes are user content) or fix the comment and add a retention sweep for expired challenges.

findings.title: Trending card 'days left' is computed but never shown; every card claims 'LIVE'
findings.evidence: athletes: '$daysLeft days left', // _TrendingRace.athletes — never rendered; card shows '$count athletes' (line 528) and a hardcoded 'LIVE' badge (line 511)
findings.detail: _challengeToTrending builds a '$daysLeft days left' string into _TrendingRace.athletes, but the card renders '$count athletes' instead — the remaining-duration info (clamped at line 85) is silently dropped, so users can't see when a race ends. Meanwhile the 'LIVE' badge is hardcoded on every card regardless of state (a race at daysLeft==0 still shows LIVE).
findings.fix: Render the days-left string on the card (there's vertical room under the athlete count) and derive the LIVE badge from c.endsAt.isAfter(now).

findings.title: User profile 'Add friend' always flips to 'Pending' even when the server auto-accepted
findings.evidence: await _friends.sendRequest(widget.userId); ... _summary = _summary?.copyWith(relationship: 'pending'); ... Text('Friend request sent')
findings.detail: FriendsService.sendRequest documents that the server may respond with status 'accepted' when the other user already sent you a request (friends_service.dart:154), and friends_screen.dart:215 handles that case ('You are now friends'). UserProfileScreen ignores the returned status: the pill shows 'Pending' and the snackbar says 'Friend request sent' even when you just became friends — and Message/posts visibility may already have changed.
findings.fix: Inspect res['status'] like friends_screen does; on 'accepted' set relationship 'friend' and show 'You are now friends'.

findings.title: Circle friends load failure shows empty states with no error or retry
findings.evidence: reportError(e, st, reason: 'circle:load-friends'); if (!mounted) return; setState(() => _loadingFriends = false);
findings.detail: If listFriends() throws (it throws on any non-200, friends_service.dart:63-65), CircleScreen just clears the spinner — the body then shows the 'No recent activity / Add friends' empty state as if the user genuinely has no friends. There is no error message, no retry, and no pull-to-refresh on this screen.
findings.fix: Track an _error flag and render ZveltErrorState with onRetry: _loadFriends (the pattern already used in FriendsScreen and ConversationsScreen).

findings.title: Participant counts fetched with N sequential HTTP calls (22s timeout each), delaying the deep-link snackbar
findings.evidence: for (final c in _challenges) { try { final result = await _challengeService.getChallengeParticipants(c.id); ... } }
findings.detail: _loadParticipantCounts awaits each challenge's participants request in series; with 10 races on a flaky connection that's worst-case minutes of serial 22s timeouts. _loadChallenges awaits this loop BEFORE _maybeOpenDeepLink() (lines 107-108), so the "You're in" confirmation snackbar for a just-joined race can appear absurdly late.
findings.fix: Use Future.wait over the challenges (each already updates state independently), and call _maybeOpenDeepLink() right after the challenge list lands instead of after the counts.

findings.title: Circle streak ring is a broken decoration — gradient ring is 8px and fully hidden behind the 64px avatar
findings.evidence: Container(padding: const EdgeInsets.all(2.5), decoration: BoxDecoration(shape: BoxShape.circle, gradient: ...), child: Container(width: 3, height: 3, ...)), _CircleAvatar(name: user.name, hue: user.hue, size: 64),
findings.detail: The first Stack child in _StreakAvatar is meant to be a gradient streak ring, but its content is a 3x3 container with 2.5px padding (~8px total), positioned at the Stack origin and completely covered by the 64px avatar drawn after it. The intended fire-gradient ring never renders — dead pixels shipped to every Top Streaks avatar.
findings.fix: Make the gradient container the parent of _CircleAvatar (padding: EdgeInsets.all(2.5), child: avatar) instead of a sibling, or delete it.

findings.title: Off-token hardcoded grays in circle screen (0xFF6B7280 text, 0xFF1A2030 divider)
findings.evidence: const Icon(Icons.search_rounded, color: Color(0xFF6B7280), size: 16), ... const Border(bottom: BorderSide(color: Color(0xFF1A2030))) (line 730)
findings.detail: circle_screen.dart repeats Color(0xFF6B7280) seven times for secondary text/icons and Color(0xFF1A2030) for row dividers instead of ZveltTokens.text2/text3 and ZveltTokens.border, drifting from the token palette the rest of the social area uses (friends_screen, conversations_screen, direct_chat_screen are all on tokens). 0xFF6B7280 on the dark surface is also lower-contrast than text2.
findings.fix: Replace with ZveltTokens.text3 (or text2) and ZveltTokens.border.

findings.title: Activity heatmap shows fabricated training data, including invented sessions on days the user never trained
findings.evidence: // Sprinkle a few softer cells in older weeks so the grid never looks // completely dead on a fresh account. final dim = [3, 10, 18, 25, 32, 40, 47].where((i) => out[i] == 0); for (final i in dim) { out[i] = 1; }
findings.detail: The 'Activity · last 8 weeks' card (lines 851-880) renders `_placeholderActivityIntensities(_streak)` — a synthetic pattern derived from the posting streak, NOT from actual workouts. Worse, lines 670-675 deliberately paint fake low-intensity cells on 7 fixed grid positions in older weeks so a fresh account 'never looks completely dead'. A brand-new user sees a heatmap claiming they trained on ~7 days they did not. This directly violates product principle #1 (never show fabricated data). ZActivityGrid even ships a ready helper `fromTrainingDays()` (z_activity_grid.dart:40) for real data that is unused here.
findings.fix: Wire StatsChartsService.getDailyTraining(days: 56) (as the comment at line 848 already plans) into ZActivityGrid.fromTrainingDays, and delete the sprinkle loop. Until real data is wired, hide the card or show an honest empty grid.

findings.title: Weekly progress card fabricates 'X / 5 Workouts' from the posting streak with a hardcoded goal
findings.evidence:
ZWeeklyProgressCard(
  done: _streak.clamp(0, 7),
  goal: 5,
  filled: _placeholderFilledWeek(_streak),
),
findings.detail: The card is labeled 'Weekly progress' / 'Workouts' (z_weekly_progress_card.dart:160) but `done` is the posting streak clamped to 7 and `filled` marks the last `_streak` calendar days as trained via `_placeholderFilledWeek` (lines 679-688) — regardless of which days the user actually worked out. The goal is hardcoded to 5 instead of trainingProfile.daysPerWeek. A user who posted 3 days in a row but trained zero times this week sees '3 / 5 Workouts' with Mon-Wed bars lit. Fabricated data on the main dashboard.
findings.fix: Derive done/filled from actual workouts this week (the /workouts API or HealthService weekly data already loaded at line 289) and read goal from TrainingProfileService().fetch().daysPerWeek.

findings.title: _initials getter throws RangeError (red-screen of home/profile) for empty or double-spaced display names
findings.evidence:
if (parts.length >= 2) return '${parts[0][0]}${parts[1][0]}'.toUpperCase();
return name.substring(0, name.length.clamp(1, 1)).toUpperCase();
findings.detail: Two crash paths, both executed during build of the home header (line 703): (1) a display name with two consecutive spaces, e.g. 'John  Doe', splits to ['John','','Doe'] and `parts[1][0]` indexes an empty string → RangeError; (2) a backend `displayName` of '' (non-null empty string, line 227 keeps it) makes `name.length` 0, `0.clamp(1,1)` returns 1, and `''.substring(0, 1)` throws. The identical bug is duplicated in skeleton_profile_tab.dart:133-138. Because it throws inside build, the entire Train tab / Profile screen renders the error widget.
findings.fix: Filter empty parts: `final parts = name.trim().split(RegExp(r'\s+')).where((p) => p.isNotEmpty).toList();` and guard `if (parts.isEmpty) return 'A';`. Fix in both files (ideally extract one shared helper).

findings.title: Recovery card tells users 'Rest day — skip the gym today' when there is zero health data behind the score
findings.detail: When HRV, sleep and resting HR are all absent (common for users whose wearable only writes steps, and always when HealthService.getSummary() errors and returns HealthSummary.empty while hasPermissions() was true), recWeight is 0 so recoveryScore is 0, which falls into the `< 40` branch (lines 334-338) and produces an authoritative training recommendation — 'Your body needs rest. Skip the gym today.' — fabricated from no signals at all. The 'Today's body' card (line 738) then shows Recovery 0% and status 'Rest day' as if measured. Related: line 328 hardcodes `recoveryAiRec = 'HRV and sleep suggest peak readiness.'` even when HRV was never measured.
findings.fix: When recWeight == 0, keep `_healthConnected` vitals but render an honest 'Not enough data for a recovery score' state instead of score 0 / 'Rest day', and make the AI-rec strings reference only the signals actually present.

findings.title: Active workout older than 8h is silently DELETEd on app start — logged sets are lost without user consent
findings.detail: discardWorkout issues DELETE /v1/workouts/{id} (workout_service.dart:305-321), destroying the draft and every set the user logged. A lifter who finishes a full evening session but forgets to tap Complete, then opens the app the next morning (>8h), has the entire workout erased with no prompt, no undo, and no notice. This is silent data loss of real training data and conflicts with the offline-first 'tracking data is sacred' posture. The 8h heuristic is reasonable for stale empty drafts, but not for drafts that contain logged sets.
findings.fix: Before silent discard, check whether the draft has logged sets (locally or via GET). If it does, either auto-complete it with the last set's timestamp or still show the resume/discard prompt regardless of age.

findings.title: Tab page cache in MainScreen does not actually preserve state — every tab switch disposes the State and refetches; the 5-minute staleness logic is dead code
findings.evidence: body: _page(_currentIndex),  // plus lines 28-52: _pageCache / _invalidateIfStale with comment 'Cached tab pages older than this on focus switch are evicted so the next [_page] call rebuilds them and they re-run their _load()'
findings.detail: Caching the Widget instance does not keep its State alive: when `body` swaps from SkeletonHomeTab to another tab, the element is unmounted and the State disposed; returning to the tab creates a fresh State whose initState re-runs `_load()` every single time. Consequences: (1) every tab switch triggers full network refetch (/me, /workouts, suggestion, etc.) and a loading flicker; (2) scroll position is lost on every switch; (3) `_invalidateIfStale` / `_staleAfter` (lines 34-52) never change behavior — eviction or not, the page reloads — so the mechanism the comments describe does not exist. The code comments show the author believes non-stale cached pages skip `_load()`, which is false.
findings.fix: Either use IndexedStack with lazy child creation (build children on first visit, keep them alive after — solves the cold-start concern from main.dart:169) or accept reload-on-switch and delete the misleading cache/staleness machinery.

findings.title: Goal Evolution shows "COULDN'T UPDATE" after the goal was in fact updated (partial-success state lies to the user)
findings.detail: _saveAndRegenerate is two steps: (1) PATCH the goal text (line 91, succeeded), (2) regenerate the weekly plan. If step 2 throws, the failed screen is titled 'COULDN'T UPDATE' (line 426) even though the goal WAS saved server-side. If the user taps Close, they leave believing nothing changed, while their stored goal now mismatches their plan/advice (coach tips still target the old goal). Also `_errorMessage` embeds raw `e.toString()` which can leak 'Exception: ...' internals to the UI.
findings.fix: On step-2 failure, use distinct copy: 'Goal saved — but the plan could not be regenerated. Retry to rebuild it.', skip re-PATCHing on retry, and strip the Exception prefix from the message.

findings.title: Units and Privacy dialogs: PATCH result unchecked / network errors escape an async void method (logged as fatal crashes, user gets no feedback)
findings.detail: _showUnitsDialog never checks the response status and has no try/catch — a 4xx/5xx silently reverts to the old unit after _load() with zero feedback. On a network exception, both _showUnitsDialog and _showPrivacyDialog (line 480) are `void ... async` so the exception is unhandled and lands in PlatformDispatcher.onError (main.dart:140-143) where it is recorded to Crashlytics with `fatal: true`, polluting the crash-free-sessions SLO while the user sees nothing. _showPrivacyDialog checks `statusCode == 200` for success but shows no error path either (line 489).
findings.fix: Wrap both PATCHes in try/catch, check statusCode, and show an error snackbar on failure (mirroring the success snackbar at line 491). Add .withTimeout() like other service calls.

findings.title: Home dashboard has no offline cache — offline cold start shows streak 0, workouts 0 and name 'Athlete' as if real
findings.evidence:
final res = await http.get(Uri.parse('$v1Base/workouts?limit=1'), ...);
if (res.statusCode != 200 || !mounted) return;  // silent return, state keeps defaults _streak=0/_totalWorkouts=0
findings.detail: _loadProfile (215) and _loadWorkouts (236) hit the network with no AppDataCache fallback (unlike TrainingProfileService, which does cache). On failure or non-200 they silently keep the initial defaults, so a user with a 40-day streak and 200 workouts who opens the app offline sees 'Streak 0 days', 'Workouts 0 total', greeting 'Athlete' — wrong data presented as real, with no offline/error indicator anywhere on the screen. This contradicts both the data-honesty and offline-first principles; the same pattern repeats in skeleton_profile_tab.dart (_StatsStrip shows 0/0/0 offline).
findings.fix: Cache the last successful /me + workouts meta in AppDataCache and render those values offline, or show an explicit 'offline — last synced …' state instead of zeros.

findings.title: Steps card says 'Connect Health' even when Health is already connected (gated on XP instead of connection)
findings.evidence: if (xp > 0) Container( ... '+$xp XP' ... ) else Text('Connect Health', ...)
findings.detail: _StepsCard receives only `steps` and `xp` (line 950-952), not `_healthConnected`. A connected user who hasn't walked enough for step XP yet (early morning: steps>0, xp==0, or 0 steps) is shown the 'Connect Health' CTA, implying their connection failed. The progress bar is also hidden for 0 steps so the connected-but-sedentary state is indistinguishable from the never-connected state.
findings.fix: Pass `_healthConnected` into _StepsCard and show 'Connect Health' only when false; when connected with xp==0 show '0 XP' or nothing.

findings.title: Hardcoded fake presence indicator: 'Status · active' + permanent green dot
findings.evidence:
Text(
  'Status · active',
  style: ZType.eyebrow.copyWith(
findings.detail: The home header always renders 'Status · active' and a green dot (lines 2419-2431) regardless of any real state — it is a decorative string masquerading as a status. Minor, but it is fabricated data on the most visible row of the app.
findings.fix: Replace with a real derived label (e.g. streak state or time-of-day greeting) or drop the 'Status' framing entirely.

findings.title: Header icon buttons and the center '+' FAB have no semantic labels for screen readers
findings.evidence:
return Semantics(
  button: true,
  child: GestureDetector(
    onTap: onTap, ... Icon(icon, color: ZveltTokens.text2, size: 18),
findings.detail: _HeaderIconButton wraps a bare Icon in Semantics(button: true) with no label, so TalkBack/VoiceOver announce 'button' for refresh, notifications (incl. its unread badge, which is purely visual) and settings. The quick-action FAB (zvelt_main_nav_bar.dart:232-236) is likewise an unlabeled Icons.add_rounded inside InkWell. Violates the accessibility principle ('semantics') for the primary navigation affordances of the app.
findings.fix: Add `label:` to the Semantics (e.g. 'Notifications, 3 unread') and a `Semantics(label: 'Quick actions', button: true)` or Tooltip around the FAB.

findings.title: Last workout card shows a chevron affordance but is not tappable
findings.evidence: const Icon(Icons.chevron_right, color: ZveltTokens.text3, size: 18),  // inside ZCard( padding: ... ) with no onTap
findings.detail: _LastWorkoutCard renders a trailing chevron_right (the universal 'tap to open' cue) but the ZCard at line 1861 passes no onTap, so tapping does nothing — a dead end. Users will expect it to open the workout detail/summary.
findings.fix: Either wire `onTap` to the workout detail screen (workout id is in the map) or remove the chevron.

findings.title: Resuming a crashed workout via the cold-start prompt never refreshes home stats; RefreshScope.home is never bumped anywhere
findings.evidence:
AppNavigator.key.currentState?.push<void>(
  MaterialPageRoute<void>(
    builder: (_) => WorkoutTrackerScreen(workoutId: ptr.workoutId),  // no onComplete
findings.detail: Other launch paths pass `onComplete: _load` so the dashboard refreshes after finishing; the resume path doesn't. The fallback channel doesn't exist either: SkeletonHomeTab subscribes to RefreshScope.home (skeleton_home_tab.dart:115-116) but `bump(RefreshScope.home)` is never called anywhere in lib/ — feed_refresh_notifier.dart:58-78 maps no push type to home. So after resuming and completing a workout the streak/total/last-workout cards stay stale until a manual pull-to-refresh or tab switch.
findings.fix: Bump RefreshScope.home from the workout-complete path (WorkoutService.completeWorkout) so all entry points refresh uniformly, then the missing onComplete becomes harmless.

findings.title: Pull-to-refresh spinner completes before steps/health/recovery and pack-challenge data actually reload
findings.detail: RefreshIndicator.onRefresh awaits `_load()` (line 696), but the health summary (steps, recovery rings, vitals) and pack challenge are fired unawaited with an extra 250ms delay, so the spinner disappears while those cards still show pre-refresh values that then pop in later. For the deliberately deferred initial load this is fine; for an explicit user refresh it reads as 'refresh didn't work'. Also note `_loadSteps` itself has no try/catch — it relies entirely on HealthService catching internally.
findings.fix: In the pull-to-refresh path only, await the steps/pack futures (Future.wait with the main batch, skipping the artificial 250ms delay).

findings.title: Profile tab hero/strip uses hardcoded off-token colors, fonts and a tier-name fallback that doesn't match the product tier ladder
findings.evidence: colors: [Color(0xFFFF5A1F), Color(0xFFFFB15C), Color(0xFFFF3D1F)],  // plus Color(0x1EFFD183)/Color(0x4CFFD183) at 640-642, divider Color(0xFF1A2030) at 704
findings.detail: The profile hero hand-rolls gradient oranges, gold chip colors and a strip divider as raw hex instead of ZveltTokens.*, and uses 'SpaceGrotesk' italic w900 directly (lines 349, 578, 618, 711) — visually a different design language from the ZCard/ZType V2 system used by the home tab it's opened from. The `_tierLabel` fallback (lines 524-532) also invents tier names ('DIAMOND ELITE', 'GOLD MEMBER') from gameLevel that match neither backend levelName semantics nor the spec ladder (Iron→…→Olympian), so users can see a tier the ranking system never granted.
findings.fix: Move colors/typography to ZveltTokens/ZType, and when levelName is missing show 'LEVEL $gameLevel' instead of a fabricated tier name.

findings.title: Settings button in profile header is a 38x38 tap target (below the 44pt minimum)
findings.evidence:
child: Container(
  width: 38,
  height: 38,
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    shape: BoxShape.circle,
findings.detail: The GestureDetector hit area equals the 38px container, under the documented 44pt/48dp minimum (CLAUDE.md touch targets). The equivalent home-header buttons correctly reserve 44px (skeleton_home_tab.dart:2500-2502). Also unlabeled for screen readers.
findings.fix: Wrap in a 44x44 SizedBox like _HeaderIconButton, and add a Semantics label or Tooltip ('Settings').

findings.title: Feed filter pills (Following/Popular) change selection but never reload the feed
findings.evidence: setState(() => _feedFilter = f.id);  // pill onTap — no _onFilterChanged()/_load() call
findings.detail: The trending/friends toggle calls _onFilterChanged() (line 888) which triggers _load(), but the All/Following/Popular pills only setState the filter id. The feed content is fetched server-side (getFeedPage(scope:..., sort:...)) and is never filtered client-side, so tapping 'Following' or 'Popular' highlights the pill while the feed keeps showing the previous filter's posts. The filter only takes effect later, as a side effect of pull-to-refresh, a push-notification refresh hint, or toggling trending/friends — which silently applies the stale pill choice.
findings.fix: In the pill onTap, replace setState(() => _feedFilter = f.id) with: _feedFilter = f.id; _onFilterChanged();

findings.title: Hide/Block/Delete/Edit do nothing visible in the main feed — onDelete/onEdit not wired
findings.evidence: (_, i) => SocialFeedPostCard(key: ValueKey(_posts[i].id), post: _posts[i], service: _feedService, onLike: () {},)  // no onDelete, no onEdit
findings.detail: SocialFeedPostCard relies on widget.onDelete to remove a post after 'Hide this post' (card line 160), 'Block user' (line 263) and 'Delete post' (line 361), and on widget.onEdit to refresh the caption after editing (line 324). The main feed passes neither. Result: hiding a post gives zero feedback and the post stays on screen; blocking a user shows a '<name> blocked' snackbar while their post remains visible (a moderation/Apple §1.2 concern); deleting your own post leaves the deleted post rendered; editing a caption shows the old caption. Everything only resolves at the next full reload. PostDetailScreen wires these correctly (post_detail_screen.dart:392-393), proving the contract exists.
findings.fix: Pass onDelete: () => setState(() => _posts.removeWhere((p) => p.id == _posts[i].id)) and onEdit: (updated) => setState(() => replace post in _posts) when constructing the card in the feed SliverList (and ideally also in BookmarksScreen).

findings.title: Like state always starts as 'not liked' — server liked-by-me state is never read
findings.evidence: bool _liked = false;  // initState only seeds _likeCount; SocialFeedPost (social_feed_post.dart:59-89) has no likedByMe field
findings.detail: The post model parses likeCount from _count but has no likedByMe/liked field, and the card hardcodes _liked = false. Every feed load, pull-to-refresh, scroll-recycle, or app restart shows an empty heart on posts the user already liked, while the like count still includes their like — the UI displays a state that is not true. Worse, tapping the heart on such a post sends the toggle endpoint, which UNLIKES it on the server; the reconciliation code (lines 394-399) then snaps the heart back to unliked and decrements the count, so the user's tap to 'like' silently removes their like.
findings.fix: Have the backend response include likedByMe (or fetch it), add it to SocialFeedPost.fromJson, and seed _liked from widget.post in initState/didUpdateWidget.

findings.title: Comments load/send have no timeout or error handling — infinite spinner and permanently disabled send when offline
findings.evidence: Future<void> _load() async { final comments = await widget.service.getComments(widget.postId); ... }  // no try/catch; service getComments/addComment (social_feed_service.dart:261-294) use plain http.get/post with no .timeout and no SocketException mapping
findings.detail: Unlike getFeedPage/toggleLike, getComments and addComment in SocialFeedService have neither .timeout nor network-exception mapping. In SocialCommentsSheet, _load() (line 831) has no try/catch, so a SocketException while offline leaves _loading=true forever (endless spinner, no retry). _send() (line 837) awaits addComment without try/catch, so a thrown network error leaves _sending=true permanently — the send button stays a disabled spinner for the rest of the sheet's life and the comment text is lost from the user's perspective. PostDetailScreen._sendComment (post_detail_screen.dart:96-115) has the same unguarded await; its _loadComments at least catches, but then renders 'No comments yet' on a network error (lines 90-93 + 413-422), which misrepresents an error as an empty state.
findings.fix: Add .timeout + SocketException/TimeoutException mapping to getComments/addComment (mirroring toggleLike), wrap _load/_send/_sendComment in try/catch/finally that resets _loading/_sending and shows a retryable error state.

findings.title: Posting a story creates a duplicate in the stories rail after the next refresh
findings.evidence: await db.update(_kTable, updates, where: 'id = ?', whereArgs: [serverId ?? story.id]);  // local row was inserted under story.id (a millis timestamp, social_plus_screen.dart:1318); serverId is the server's UUID — update matches 0 rows
findings.detail: addStory inserts a local row keyed by the client-generated id (DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch, social_plus_screen.dart:1318) and then, on a successful POST, tries to write the server's imageUrl/expiresAt into the row WHERE id = serverId — a row that does not exist, so the update is a no-op. On the next loadActiveStories, the server feed returns the same story under its UUID and _upsertRemote inserts it as a second row; _loadLocal then returns both (lines 304-308), so the user's own story appears twice in the rail — once as 'You' (local file) and once under their display name (remote URL) — for the full 24h window. The server's authoritative expiresAt also never lands on the local row, defeating the clock-rollback defense the code documents.
findings.fix: When the server returns an id, re-key the local row: db.update(... where id = story.id, set id = serverId, image_url, expires_at) or delete the local row and insert under serverId.

findings.title: Story images bypass mediaAbsoluteUrl — relative /uploads/ paths from the backend won't load
findings.evidence: return ZveltNetworkImage(url: url, ...)  // raw story.imageUrl; contrast with post images: ZveltNetworkImage(url: mediaAbsoluteUrl(post.imageUrl)) at social_feed_post_card.dart:632
findings.detail: SocialFeedPost.imageUrl is documented as 'Cale relativă /uploads/... sau URL absolut' and both the feed card and the gallery wrap it with mediaAbsoluteUrl (api_config.dart:16-22) before handing it to CachedNetworkImage. The story thumb (_StoryThumb, line 1208) and the full-screen story viewer (line 1583) pass story.imageUrl raw. If the stories endpoint returns the same relative-path convention as posts, friends' stories (which have no local file or base64 fallback) silently degrade to a blank/gradient placeholder, and the viewer shows a black screen for 5 seconds per story.
findings.fix: Wrap both call sites: ZveltNetworkImage(url: mediaAbsoluteUrl(story.imageUrl), ...).

findings.title: Push refresh hint and pull-to-refresh blank the entire feed to a full-screen spinner
findings.evidence: setState(() { _loading = true; ... });  // _load(); body: _loading ? Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator(...)) : ... (line 433)
findings.detail: _load() unconditionally sets _loading=true, and the build swaps the whole CustomScrollView for a centered spinner while loading. _onFeedRefreshHint (line 152-155) calls _load() whenever FeedRefreshNotifier bumps the feed scope — which happens on every post_like/post_comment/friend push notification (feed_refresh_notifier.dart:60-69). So a friend liking your post while you're reading mid-feed wipes the screen to a spinner and resets your scroll position to the top. Pull-to-refresh has the same flash: the RefreshIndicator's own spinner is immediately replaced by the full-screen one.
findings.fix: Only show the full-screen spinner on first load (_loading && _posts.isEmpty); for refreshes keep the current list rendered while the new page loads, then swap data in place.

findings.title: Bookmark button shows 'saved' after a failed request, and a failed un-bookmark removes the row in BookmarksScreen
findings.evidence: } catch (e, st) { reportError(...); if (mounted) setState(() => _bookmarked = !_bookmarked); }  // no optimistic flip ever happened before the await
findings.detail: The bookmark action button never optimistically flips state — it only sets _bookmarked from the server response on success. The catch branch nonetheless inverts _bookmarked, so a network failure (toggleBookmark has no SocketException handling; the 22s timeout throws) flips the icon to the target state without the server having changed anything, and shows no error message. Separately, SocialFeedService.toggleBookmark returns false on any non-2xx (social_feed_service.dart:391-393), so when a server error occurs while toggling an already-bookmarked post, the card receives false, sets the icon to un-bookmarked, and fires onBookmarkChanged(false) — in BookmarksScreen that animates the row out (bookmarks_screen.dart:118-131) even though the post is still bookmarked server-side.
findings.fix: Remove the state flip in the catch (show a snackbar instead, like the menu path does), and make toggleBookmark throw SocialFeedException on non-2xx instead of returning false.

findings.title: Notifications header reads 'EARLIER TODAY' for all notifications regardless of age
findings.evidence: _buildSectionLabel('EARLIER TODAY'),  // unconditional, rendered above week-old items and even above the empty state
findings.detail: The single hardcoded section label claims every notification — including ones from days or weeks ago (cards themselves show '3d ago', 'Mar 14') — happened 'earlier today'. It is also rendered above the 'No notifications yet' empty state, labeling nothing. This directly contradicts the timestamps shown two lines below it on each card and fabricates a recency the data doesn't have.
findings.fix: Either group items into real sections (Today / This week / Earlier) from createdAt, or drop the label entirely.

findings.title: Notification timestamps render double suffix: '2h ago ago' / 'just now ago'
findings.evidence: Text('$timeAgo ago', ...)  // timeAgo comes from safeRelativeTime (line 280), which already returns '${n}m ago' / '${n}h ago' / 'just now' (relative_time.dart:26-29)
findings.detail: safeRelativeTime returns strings that already include 'ago' ('5m ago', '2h ago') or are not 'ago'-compatible at all ('just now', 'Mar 14'). The card appends ' ago' again, producing '2h ago ago', 'just now ago', and 'Mar 14 ago' for week-old items. Visible on every notification card.
findings.fix: Change to Text(timeAgo, ...).

findings.title: Notifications service maps any non-200 to an empty page — server errors render 'No notifications yet'
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode != 200) { return const NotificationsPage(items: [], hasMore: false); }
findings.detail: listPage swallows 401/403/500 responses as a successful empty page, so NotificationsScreen shows the 'No notifications yet — Your activity will appear here.' empty state during a server outage or auth expiry instead of its existing error state with retry (the error path is only reachable for thrown exceptions). The user is told they have no activity when in fact the app failed to fetch it. Also note neither listPage nor unreadCount has a .timeout, so the screen's spinner can hang on the platform default.
findings.fix: Throw NotificationsException on non-200 (the screen already handles errors via _error + ZveltErrorState), and add .timeout to listPage/unreadCount.

findings.title: Gallery shows 'No posts yet — Be the first to share your progress!' on network/server errors
findings.evidence: } catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'gallery:load-posts'); if (mounted) { setState(() { _loading = false; _hasMore = false; }); } }  // then line 289: if (!_loading && _posts.isEmpty) return ZveltEmptyState(title: 'No posts yet', ...)
findings.detail: Both the catch branch and the non-200 branch (lines 110-116) collapse failures into the same state as a genuinely empty gallery. Offline users see the cheerful 'Be the first to share your progress!' empty state with no error indication and no retry affordance (there is also no pull-to-refresh on the grid), a direct violation of the 'honest empty/connect state' principle.
findings.fix: Track an _error flag and render ZveltErrorState with onRetry (the pattern already used by BookmarksScreen), plus wrap the grid in RefreshIndicator.

findings.title: Gallery filter tap is silently dropped while a page fetch is in flight
findings.evidence: Future<void> _loadPosts({bool reset = false}) async { if (_fetchingMore) return;  // reset call from the filter pill is discarded
findings.detail: Tapping Recent/Popular/Mine calls _loadPosts(reset: true), but the in-flight guard returns immediately if a pagination fetch is running (easy to trigger — the scroll listener prefetches at maxScrollExtent-200). The pill updates visually while the grid keeps the old filter's content, and nothing ever retries. There is also no stale-response guard (no generation counter like the feed's _loadGen), so a slow first-filter response can land after a quick second switch and overwrite the newer content.
findings.fix: Adopt the _loadGen generation-counter pattern from SocialPlusScreen: let reset bump the generation and cancel/ignore the in-flight page instead of dropping the reset.

findings.title: Gallery tiles are not tappable — dead end with no route to the post
findings.evidence: return ClipRRect(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4), child: Stack(...));  // no GestureDetector/InkWell, post id is available in _posts[i]['id']
findings.detail: The grid shows photos with like-count badges, but tapping a tile does nothing. The normalised post map carries 'id' (line 141) and PostDetailScreen(postId:) exists and is used from notifications, so the navigation target is one push away. As a fresh user, the explore surface invites browsing and then refuses every interaction.
findings.fix: Wrap the tile in GestureDetector(onTap: () => Navigator.push(... PostDetailScreen(postId: post['id']))).

findings.title: Feed controls fall below the 44pt minimum touch target
findings.evidence: AnimatedContainer(duration: ..., height: 30, ...)  // filter pills; trending toggle height: 34 (line 874), '+ NEW' height: 26 (line 995), '+ POST' height: 28 (line 1069)
findings.detail: The CLAUDE.md accessibility rule mandates >=44pt touch targets. The feed filter pills (30px), trending/friends segmented toggle (34px), '+ NEW' challenge button (26px) and '+ POST' button (28px) are all bare GestureDetectors with no padded hit area, no minimum tap size, and no Semantics(button:) annotation — they're invisible to screen readers as actionable controls. The gallery filter pills (gallery_screen.dart:236) have the same 30px height. By contrast, the post card's overflow button correctly wraps in a 44x44 SizedBox + Semantics.
findings.fix: Wrap these in a min-44pt constrained tappable (or add vertical hit-test padding) and Semantics(button: true, selected: sel).

findings.title: Post card like/comment counts go stale after in-sheet actions
findings.evidence: label: '${post.commentCount}',  // never updated after SocialCommentsSheet adds a comment; sheet has no result callback
findings.detail: Adding a comment via the bottom sheet refreshes the sheet's own list but the card underneath keeps the old commentCount until a full feed reload. In the main feed the onLike callback is an empty closure (social_plus_screen.dart:495), so nothing reconciles counts there either.
findings.fix: Have SocialCommentsSheet pop with the new count (or accept an onCommentAdded callback) and setState a local _commentCount in the card, mirroring _likeCount.

findings.title: Story composer save has no error handling — oversized image leaves the sheet stuck on a spinner
findings.evidence: await _service.addStory(story); if (!mounted) return; Navigator.of(context).pop(true);  // addStory throws StateError('Story image too large...') for >5MB (social_story_service.dart:357-360); _save has no try/catch and never resets _saving
findings.detail: If addStory throws (the >5MB guard is the documented throwing path), _saving stays true forever, the 'Publish story' button remains a disabled spinner, and the user gets no message. Low likelihood since the picker re-encodes at 1280px/q75, but the failure mode is a hard dead end when hit.
findings.fix: Wrap the addStory call in try/catch/finally; reset _saving and show a snackbar on failure.

findings.title: Stories have no audience indicator — unclear who can see a published story
findings.evidence: const Text('New story (24h)', ...)  // composer offers caption + location fields but no visibility control or audience disclosure; posts get _PrivacyBadge (social_feed_post_card.dart:1022) per the privacy-by-default rule
findings.detail: Every post carries a PUBLIC/FRIENDS/PRIVATE badge with a tooltip, honoring 'users must see at a glance who can read each post'. Stories — which include an optional location field, arguably more sensitive — are published with no audience label in the composer and none in the viewer. The user cannot tell whether their gym photo + location goes to friends or everyone.
findings.fix: Add a one-line audience disclosure to the composer ('Visible to your friends for 24h') matching whatever the /v1/stories endpoint actually enforces, and ideally the same badge in the story viewer chrome.

findings.title: Hardcoded gray Color(0xFF6B7280) used instead of ZveltTokens.text3 across feed and notifications labels
findings.evidence: color: sel ? Colors.white : const Color(0xFF6B7280),  // also lines 987, 1061; notifications_screen.dart lines 308, 332, 473
findings.detail: Six call sites in this area hardcode #6B7280 where ZveltTokens.text3 (#939893) or text2 (#5F6360) is the system color. Besides the token drift, the unselected segmented-toggle label is 9.5px w900 #6B7280 on bg2 (#EEF1ED) which computes to roughly 4.0:1 — below the 4.5:1 WCAG AA threshold for small text.
findings.fix: Replace with ZveltTokens.text2/text3; for the toggle's unselected label use text2 to clear 4.5:1.

findings.title: Header 'YOUR TRIBE · 0 TRAINED TODAY' shows a fabricated zero while loading or after a failed fetch
findings.evidence: 'YOUR TRIBE · $_friendsTrainedToday TRAINED TODAY'  // _friendsTrainedToday = 0 sentinel (line 59); _loadFriendActivity silently returns on non-200/exception (lines 255, 263-265)
findings.detail: The same '0 is ambiguous' problem the team explicitly fixed for the race hero participant count (the _heroLoading flag, lines 61-72) exists one widget above it: when /v1/friends/activity fails or hasn't resolved, the header asserts '0 TRAINED TODAY' as fact. A user with active friends and a flaky connection is told nobody trained.
findings.fix: Track a nullable count; render the label without the count segment (just 'YOUR TRIBE') until a real value arrives — same pattern as _heroLoading.

findings.title: Delete account screen is white-on-white: nearly all text (title, warnings, deletion list, typed confirmation) is invisible
findings.evidence: style: const TextStyle(color: Colors.white, ...) on TextField, with fillColor: ZveltTokens.surface (#FFFFFF) and Scaffold backgroundColor: ZveltTokens.bg (#F6F7F5)
findings.detail: The screen was written for the old dark theme but ZveltTokens is now the V2 LIGHT system (zvelt_tokens.dart:21 bg=#F6F7F5, :25 surface=#FFFFFF). Every hardcoded Colors.white text is invisible on the light background: AppBar back chevron (line 111) and 'Delete account' title (line 116), 'This is permanent' (139), 'What gets deleted' header (152-154) and all six item titles (178), 'Type DELETE to confirm' (214-216), the _TimelineRow body text (312), and worst of all the confirmation TextField input itself (225) — the user types DELETE and sees nothing appear in the field. This is the Play-policy/GDPR-required deletion path and it is unreadable end to end; the only legible elements are the red CTA and gray hint text.
findings.fix: Replace every Colors.white with ZveltTokens.text (and ZveltTokens.text2 where secondary) throughout the screen; verify against the light token palette.

findings.title: Appearance setting (Dark / Light / Auto) is a placebo — both theme pointers resolve to the same light theme
findings.evidence: /// Theme alias ... V2 is light-only, both pointers resolve to the same theme data.\n  static ThemeData get lightThemeData => themeData;\n  static ThemeData get darkThemeData => themeData;
findings.detail: Settings exposes a SegmentedButton with Dark/Light/Auto (settings_screen.dart:1325-1344) that persists a ThemeMode via ZveltThemeNotifier, but main.dart wires theme: AppTheme.lightThemeData and darkTheme: AppTheme.themeData (main.dart:275-276) which are the SAME ThemeData, and virtually every screen hardcodes const ZveltTokens light colors anyway. Selecting 'Dark' visibly does nothing. The toggle persists but has zero effect — exactly the dead-toggle pattern the owner cares about.
findings.fix: Either remove the Appearance tile until a real dark theme exists, or build a dark ZveltTokens variant and make darkThemeData actually dark.

findings.title: Language setting does nothing — pref is written but never read; snack falsely promises 'restart the app to apply'
findings.evidence: _snack('Language updated — restart the app to apply');  // but grep shows 'zvelt_language' is only read back by this same screen, and app_strings.dart:1 says 'App copy in English. Replace with Localizations when adding language picker.'
findings.detail: The Language dialog offers 10 languages (Español, 日本語, العربية, ...) and stores the choice in SharedPreferences, but no localization machinery exists: MaterialApp has no locale/localizationsDelegates wired to it and lib/l10n/app_strings.dart is English-only. Restarting changes nothing. A user who picks 日本語 is told the change will apply after restart — that is fabricated behavior.
findings.fix: Remove the Language tile (or restrict it to 'English' with a 'more coming soon' note) until l10n is actually wired into MaterialApp.

findings.title: Physical data screen shows fabricated defaults (75 kg / 175 cm / male) when the profile load fails, and Save would overwrite real data with them
findings.evidence: double _weightKg = 75;\n  double _heightCm = 175;\n  String _sex = 'male'; ... } catch (e) { debugPrint('Physical data load error: $e'); }
findings.detail: _load() swallows any network/HTTP failure with a debugPrint (lines 70-72) and falls through to rendering the editor with the hardcoded defaults, presented as if they were the user's saved values. There is no error/retry state. Worse, if the user taps Save, PATCH /me/profile (line 81-86) persists 75 kg/175 cm/male to the backend, silently overwriting their real bodyweight — which feeds the SR/ranking calculation.
findings.fix: On load failure show an explicit error state with Retry instead of the editor; only show sliders once real values (or a true first-time-setup state) are known.

findings.title: Profile tab Units dialog: PATCH has no error handling, no status check, no timeout — selection silently fails offline
findings.detail: Unlike the Settings screen's units flow (settings_screen.dart:654-661 which checks the result and shows an error snack), the profile tab's _showUnitsDialog never checks res.statusCode and is not wrapped in try/catch inside an async void method — a network failure throws an unhandled async exception, and a 4xx/5xx silently reverts the radio after _load() with zero feedback. Offline-first principle: the user believes they switched to imperial but nothing persisted. Same defect in _showPrivacyDialog (line 480): no try/catch, and non-200 produces no message at all (only the 200 path shows 'Privacy updated').
findings.fix: Reuse ProfileService.updateProfile (which throws typed errors) or replicate the Settings _patchProfile pattern: timeout + status check + error snack; ideally delete this duplicate dialog and route to the Settings one.

findings.title: Birth year picker: tapping Done without scrolling saves null even though the wheel visibly shows a selected year
findings.evidence: int? picked = birthYear;  ... onSelectedItemChanged: (i) => picked = years[i],
findings.detail: When birthYear is null the wheel is initialized to (currentYear - 25) which is visually highlighted as selected, but `picked` is only updated by onSelectedItemChanged, which does not fire for the initial position. A user who opens the sheet, sees their year already centered, and taps Done gets null saved — 'Optional' remains and they think it saved.
findings.fix: Initialize `picked = years[initIndex]` when opening the sheet (or read ctrl.selectedItem on Done).

findings.title: Physical data screen: signed-out token leaves an infinite loading spinner
findings.evidence: final token = await _auth.getAccessToken();\n      if (token == null) return;  // returns from _load(), skipping line 73's setState(() => _loading = false)
findings.detail: The early `return` inside the try block exits _load() entirely, so `if (mounted) setState(() => _loading = false);` at line 73 never runs. If the access token is null/expired-unrefreshable the screen shows a CircularProgressIndicator forever with no way out except back.
findings.fix: Use a guard that still clears _loading (e.g. set an auth-error state) instead of a bare return.

findings.title: Settings initial load has no HTTP timeouts and gates the whole screen — a hung request shows an eternal spinner
findings.evidence:
final res = await http.get(
        Uri.parse('$v1Base/me'),
        headers: {'Authorization': 'Bearer $token'},
      );  // no .timeout/.withTimeout, same at line 214 (_loadIntegrations)
findings.detail: _load() (line 105-116) awaits Future.wait of six loaders and only then clears _loading which gates the entire ListView. _loadProfile (line 180) and _loadIntegrations (line 214) use plain http.get with no timeout, so a stalled connection (the codebase itself notes 'a slow Render dyno' at line 295) blocks Settings — including Log out and Delete account — indefinitely. The team already added a 30s timeout to _patchProfile for exactly this reason but not to the reads.
findings.fix: Add .timeout(...) to both reads (they're already try/caught), or render the static tiles immediately and load remote state per-section.

findings.title: Two-factor toggle fails silently on non-200 responses
findings.detail: _toggleTwoFa only handles the 200 path; a 400/409/500 from POST /auth/2fa/{action} produces no snack and no state change — the user confirms the dialog and nothing happens, with no explanation. Only thrown exceptions reach the catch with its error snack.
findings.fix: Add an else branch that decodes the error body and calls _snack(..., isError: true), like the change-password dialog does.

findings.title: Integration sync and connect fail silently on non-200; Sync spinner just stops
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode == 200 && mounted) { ... _snack(...); await _loadIntegrations(); }  // non-200 → nothing; same pattern in _connect at line 320
findings.detail: _syncIntegration shows 'Imported N activities' on 200 but a 401/429/500 ends with the loading indicator disappearing and zero feedback. _connect (line 316-324) likewise does nothing visible if /integrations/{provider}/auth-url returns non-200 — the Connect button appears dead.
findings.fix: Add explicit non-200 handling with an error snack in both methods.

findings.title: OAuth connect flows never refresh after returning from the browser — tile still says 'Connect' after a successful authorization
findings.evidence: await launchUrl(uri, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);  // end of _connectStrava; _SettingsScreenState has no WidgetsBindingObserver / didChangeAppLifecycleState
findings.detail: Strava (and garmin/fitbit/polar/wahoo via _connect) hand off to an external browser, but when the user returns to the app nothing re-fetches _strava/_connected. The tile keeps showing 'Strava — Connect' until the user backs out of Settings and re-enters, which reads as 'the connection failed'.
findings.fix: Add WidgetsBindingObserver and re-run _loadStravaStatus/_loadIntegrations on AppLifecycleState.resumed (or poll once after launch).

findings.title: Profile tab shows bodyweight in kg even when the user has chosen imperial units
findings.evidence: subtitle: _weightKg != null\n                          ? '${_weightKg!.toStringAsFixed(1)} kg'\n                          : 'Not set',
findings.detail: _unitSystem is loaded into state (line 79) and used for the Units row label two tiles below (line 221), but the Physical data subtitle hardcodes kg. An imperial user sees 'Units: Imperial (lbs, in)' directly under '74.8 kg'.
findings.fix: Convert with the same formula used in profile_screen.dart's _weightDisplay (kg * 2.20462, show 'lbs') when _unitSystem == 'imperial'.

findings.title: Two divergent editors for the same privacyDefault field with contradictory explanations
findings.evidence: title: const Text('Default post privacy'), ... jsonEncode({'privacyDefault': result})  // vs settings_screen.dart:558 'Profile visibility' / 'Anyone can view your profile' patching the same field
findings.detail: Settings' 'Profile visibility' dialog (settings_screen.dart:556-560) describes privacyDefault as who can view your profile ('Public — Anyone can view your profile'), while the profile tab's 'Privacy' dialog describes the same backend field as default post privacy ('who sees your activity'). The user can change it in either place and the other screen's stale label only updates on reload; more importantly the two screens give the user conflicting mental models of what this privacy control actually governs — bad for a privacy-by-default product.
findings.fix: Keep one editor (Settings), align the copy with what the backend actually enforces, and make the profile tab row deep-link to it.

findings.title: heatmap_screen.dart is unreachable dead code, in Romanian, with dark-on-dark broken token usage
findings.evidence: grep 'HeatmapScreen' across lib/ matches only the defining file; UI strings 'ACTIVITĂȚI', 'Se încarcă rutele…', 'Nicio activitate înregistrată'; title uses color: ZveltTokens.text (#111111) on backgroundColor: _kBgDark (#0A0A0F)
findings.detail: No navigation route anywhere pushes HeatmapScreen (not in the already-flagged dead list). If it were ever wired up it would also be broken: it pairs hardcoded dark backgrounds with the now-light ZveltTokens.text/text values (e.g. 'MY ROUTES' at line 226, empty-card text at lines 691/701 are near-black on near-black) and ships Romanian copy in an English app.
