findings.fix: Delete the file, or fix tokens + localize before wiring it into the profile tab.

findings.title: IntegrationsScreen ('Connected apps') is only reachable from the already-dead AccountSettingsScreen — duplicate of Settings integrations
findings.evidence: grep 'IntegrationsScreen(' → only screens/profile/account_settings_screen.dart:287 (known dead) and the defining file
findings.detail: The live integrations UI lives in settings_screen.dart. This separate screen duplicates the connect/sync/disconnect logic (with slightly different error handling, e.g. raw 'Error: $e' snack at line 134) and will drift further; it is dead weight maintained alongside the real one.
findings.fix: Delete it together with account_settings_screen.dart, or extract one shared integrations widget.

findings.title: Achievements list has no empty state — a user with zero achievements gets a blank list under '0 / 0' chips
findings.evidence: child: ListView.separated(\n  padding: ..., itemCount: _achievements.length,  // no branch for _achievements.isEmpty
findings.detail: If the backend returns an empty achievements array (200 OK), the screen renders the two summary chips ('Unlocked 0', 'XP earned 0') over an empty scroll area with no explanation of how achievements are earned. Honest, but a dead end for a new user.
findings.fix: Add an empty-state card ('Complete your first workout to start unlocking achievements').

findings.title: Achievements auth-error CTA says 'Sign in' but only pops to the root route without signing the user in or out
findings.detail: On a 401/403 the error card shows 'Please sign in again' with a 'Sign in' button, but tapping it just dismisses back to the home tab — the user is still in their (broken) session and no login screen appears. Dead-end promise.
findings.fix: Route through the app's logout/re-auth path (e.g. trigger the AuthGate flow) instead of popUntil.

findings.title: Sub-44pt touch targets: integration card buttons (~31px) and profile avatar edit pencil (34px)
findings.evidence: padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 14, vertical: 8), ... minimumSize: Size.zero,
findings.detail: The Connect/Sync/Disconnect buttons in _IntegrationCard explicitly zero out minimumSize and use 8px vertical padding (~31px tall, also lines 1669/1680), and the avatar edit button in skeleton_profile_tab.dart:592-593 is a 34x34 GestureDetector. Both fall under the project's 44pt/48dp non-negotiable minimum.
findings.fix: Remove minimumSize: Size.zero / set minimumSize: Size(0, 44); wrap the pencil in a 44x44 hit area.

findings.title: Tier badge: pale gold text (#FFD183) on near-white background fails WCAG contrast badly
findings.evidence: color: Color(0xFFFFD183),  // 10px w900 text inside Color(0x1EFFD183) chip on ZveltTokens.bg (#F6F7F5)
findings.detail: The 'IRON MEMBER'/tier label renders 10px light-gold text on a 12%-alpha gold chip over the light page background — roughly 1.4:1 contrast, effectively invisible in sunlight. Another dark-theme leftover; same applies to the chip border 0x4CFFD183. The _StatsStrip divider Color(0xFF1A2030) at line 704 is the inverse leftover (harsh dark navy hairline on a white card).
findings.fix: Use a darkened gold (e.g. #8A6116) for text/icon on the light tint, and ZveltTokens.border for the stats divider.

findings.title: Edit profile bottom sheet is not scrollable — overflow with keyboard open on small screens
findings.evidence: child: Column(\n        mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, ...  // 3 TextFields (bio maxLines:3) + button, no SingleChildScrollView
findings.detail: _EditProfileSheet pads by viewInsets.bottom but the content Column has no scroll container. With the keyboard up on a small device (or textScaler at the 1.3 cap), name+username+bio+button (~480px) plus keyboard exceeds the viewport and produces a RenderFlex overflow, clipping the Save button.
findings.fix: Wrap the Column in SingleChildScrollView (and consider DraggableScrollableSheet since isScrollControlled is already true).

findings.title: Settings privacy toggles: concurrent _loadPrefs/_loadProfile race can overwrite fresh server values with stale cache
findings.evidence:
await Future.wait([
      _loadProfile(),
      ...
      _loadPrefs(),  // both setState the same _feedFriendsOnly/_discoveryOptIn/_dmFriendsOnly fields
findings.detail: _loadProfile (server truth, then mirrors to prefs) and _loadPrefs (cached values) run in parallel and write the same three toggle fields. Normally prefs resolve first, but if SharedPreferences I/O is slow the cached values can land after the server values and the toggles render stale state for the whole session — a wrong-looking privacy setting. Also a stray trailing _Divider sits after the lone Export tile at line 1020 (visible orphan rule at the card bottom).
findings.fix: Load prefs first, then fetch the server state sequentially (prefs are only a cold-start hint anyway); drop the trailing _Divider.

findings.title: Token refresh wipes the session on ANY non-200, including transient 5xx
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode != 200) { await _clearTokens(); return null; }
findings.detail: A 500/502/503 from the backend (the code itself documents Render free-tier cold starts of ~60s) permanently deletes both tokens and logs the user out. Session destruction should only happen on 401/403 (refresh token actually rejected); on 5xx the refresh token is still valid and should be kept for a later retry.
findings.fix: Only _clearTokens() when statusCode is 401/403; on 5xx/timeout return null without clearing so the next attempt can succeed.

findings.title: Offline launch with expired access token shows the logged-out Welcome/Login screen (offline-first violation)
findings.evidence: // Access expired or missing — try refresh to verify session is still active ... final refreshed = await _refreshAccessToken(); return refreshed != null;
findings.detail: hasValidToken() requires a successful network refresh when the access token is expired. _refreshAccessToken's catch (line 244) returns null on network failure, so hasValidToken returns false and AuthGate (main.dart:333/440) renders WelcomeScreen/LoginScreen. A signed-in user opening the app offline (e.g. a basement gym) is locked out of workout tracking entirely, breaking the offline-first principle. Worse, the gate can also block up to httpTimeout=75s on a cold-start refresh before showing anything past the spinner.
findings.fix: Treat 'refresh token present + refresh failed due to network' as logged-in (optimistic offline session); only treat the session as dead when the server explicitly rejects the refresh token.

findings.title: Welcome 'Get started' never passes startInSignup — new users land on the Sign In form
findings.evidence: return WelcomeScreen(onGetStarted: _completeWelcomeEntry, onAlreadyHaveAccount: _completeWelcomeEntry); ... return LoginScreen(onLoggedIn: ...)
findings.detail: LoginScreen has a documented startInSignup parameter (login_screen.dart:13-16: "Welcome's 'Get started' passes true so new users land directly on the signup form") but AuthGate builds LoginScreen without it, and both Welcome callbacks are identical. 'GET STARTED' and 'I ALREADY HAVE AN ACCOUNT' do exactly the same thing; new users must discover the small 'No account? Sign up' toggle — the exact bug the parameter was written to fix.
findings.fix: Track which Welcome CTA was tapped in AuthGate state and pass startInSignup: true to LoginScreen for the 'Get started' path.

findings.title: Step 20 asserts a fabricated personal rank '#4,829 · CHALLENGER · TIER III' with fake neighbors
findings.evidence: const TextSpan(text: 'You\'re starting\nat '), WidgetSpan(child: _GT('#4,829', fontSize: 32)) ... (4827, 'James_S', '+2', false), (4828, 'Marco_L', '+5', false), (4829, name, '', true)
findings.detail: The screen states as fact that THIS user holds rank #4,829 in a 'Hall of Fame', surrounded by hardcoded fake athletes with fake rank deltas. No ranking call is made; the number is invented. This directly violates principle #1 (never show fabricated data) and #4 (explainability — there is no 'why' behind the rank because there is no rank).
findings.fix: Either fetch a real starting placement from the ranking service, or reframe honestly ('Everyone starts unranked — your first workout places you').

findings.title: Step 21 invents '3 athletes match your fingerprint' and points users to a screen where they don't exist
findings.evidence: static const _matches = [('Marco K.', 'Berlin', '94%', 'Bench 105kg · Squat 145kg', '2.3km', 1), ...] ... TextSpan(text: 'Feed → The Circle', ...) ' once you\'re inside.'
findings.detail: Hardcoded fake people with fabricated match percentages, PRs and distances ('2.3km' away) are presented as real matches computed from the user's data, and the user is explicitly told to find them in 'Feed → The Circle'. They will not be there — a guaranteed broken promise/dead end in the first session, plus implied proximity matching the app never did.
findings.fix: Remove or replace with a real friend-matching call; if matching isn't built, describe the feature without naming fake matched people.

findings.title: Fabricated community/efficacy statistics throughout the hero steps (84,000 athletes, 2,847 this week, 78% stall, median climbs 60%)
findings.evidence:
WidgetSpan(child: _GT('84,000', fontSize: 40)), const TextSpan(text: '
athletes who
refuse to plateau.') ... TextSpan(text: '2,847 athletes', ...) ' joined this week alone.' ... _GT('78%') ' of lifters
stall within
18 months.' ... 'Median Zvelt athlete climbs 60%'
findings.detail: Step 02 (line 666), Step 04 (lines 885, 924), Step 07 (line 1243) and Step 20 (line 3305) state specific invented numbers as fact for a pre-launch app with no users. Beyond the owner's data-honesty principle, these are also legally risky marketing claims (app store policy on deceptive claims).
findings.fix: Remove the specific numbers or replace with real metrics once available; the named fake testimonial grid in Step 04 (_tribe, line 864) should go too.

findings.title: Onboarding back button chevron is white on a near-white background — effectively invisible
findings.evidence: color: Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.04), ... child: const Icon(Icons.chevron_left_rounded, color: Colors.white, size: 18)
findings.detail: ZveltTokens is now the V2 LIGHT system (bg = #F6F7F5, zvelt_tokens.dart:21) but the onboarding shell still uses dark-theme assumptions: a white icon in a circle filled with white@4% on a cream page. The back affordance on all 28 steps is essentially invisible, so users can't see how to correct an answer. Same dark-theme leftover at line 276 (unfilled progress segments white@7% — the progress track is invisible) and line 2049.
findings.fix: Use ZveltTokens.text/text2 for the chevron and ZveltTokens.surface3 for unfilled segments.

findings.title: Step 02 chart: the 'EVERYONE ELSE' comparison line and label are painted white on a pure-white card
findings.evidence: ..color = Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.3) ... _label(canvas, s.width - 4, s.height * 0.6, 'EVERYONE ELSE', Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.4));
findings.detail: _PlateauPainter draws the dashed plateau curve, its label and end dot in translucent white, but the chart card is ZveltTokens.surface = #FFFFFF (line 674). The whole point of the chart — Zvelt curve vs the plateau — is lost because the comparison line is invisible. Grid lines at line 702 (white@4%) are also gone.
findings.fix: Paint the plateau line/label with ZveltTokens.text3/text4 and grid lines with ZveltTokens.hairline.

findings.title: brand3 (#FFB088) used as a text color on light backgrounds — ~1.8:1 contrast across many steps
findings.evidence: _Eyebrow('Feature 01 · AI Coach', color: ZveltTokens.brand3) ... Text('${(_progress * 100).round()}% COMPLETE', ... color: ZveltTokens.brand3
findings.detail: ZveltTokens.brand3 = #FFB088 (light peach) was a readable accent on the old dark theme but fails WCAG hard on bg #F6F7F5/white (~1.8:1, below even the 3:1 large-text bar). It is used for eyebrows (949, 1052, 1175, 1279, 1382...), the loading percentage (3125), painter labels ('ZVELT ATHLETES', 727), match details (3374), tribe results (907), 'TIER' rank text (3284) and more — mostly at 8-12px. Also: gold '#1' rank #FFD56A on white (1214) and ZveltTokens.info #7BC6FF for the Login 'No account? Sign up' link (login_screen.dart:219, ~1.9:1).
findings.fix: Use brand/brandDeep for accent text on light surfaces; reserve brand3 for fills/halos.

findings.title: Welcome 'I ALREADY HAVE AN ACCOUNT' is 12px gray #9CA3AF on white — ~2.5:1 contrast
findings.evidence: fontSize: 12, fontWeight: FontWeight.w600, letterSpacing: 0.85, color: const Color(0xFF9CA3AF)
findings.detail: The only path to login for returning users is a tiny low-contrast text button (fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text). Welcome also hardcodes off-token colors (_ctaBlue #2563EB, cyan/purple gradient, lines 18-20) that don't exist in ZveltTokens — a different accent system from the rest of the app.
findings.fix: Bump size/contrast (e.g. #4B5563) and consider aligning Welcome's palette with ZveltTokens.

findings.title: Touch targets below 44pt and no button semantics across onboarding controls
findings.evidence: child: Container(width: 34, height: 34, ... ) // back button; AnimatedContainer(... width: 42, height: 24, // permission toggle (line 2029)
findings.detail: The back button is 34x34 and the per-permission toggle switch is 42x24, both under the 44pt/48dp minimum. Nearly every interactive element in the flow (selection cards, CTAs, toggles, the _OnbCta itself) is a bare GestureDetector + Container with no Semantics(button: true)/selected state, so TalkBack/VoiceOver users hear plain text with no indication anything is tappable or which option is selected.
findings.fix: Wrap tappables in Semantics(button: true, selected: ...) or use InkWell/Material buttons, and pad small targets to >=44pt.

findings.title: Final 'Press + to begin' CTA can silently block for minutes re-running the full backend sync
findings.evidence: Future<void> _finish() async { if (!_p._synced) { ... await OnboardingService().completeOnboarding(_p.toPayload()); ...
findings.detail: _p._synced is only true when result.isSuccess (profile AND settings synced, line 2993). If the settings PATCH failed in _Step18 (e.g. 500), the flow still advances (gate at line 3008 only requires profileSynced), and then _finish re-runs the ENTIRE completeOnboarding — including a fresh AI plan generation (the prewarm was consumed) — awaited inline on the final 'Press + to begin' tap with zero loading UI on _Step24 (line 3696). The button looks dead for up to 75s+; nothing prevents double-taps firing concurrent syncs either.
findings.fix: Show a progress/disabled state on the final CTA while _finish runs, guard against re-entry, and skip plan regeneration in the fallback sync (only retry the failed PATCHes).

findings.title: Backing up from Step 19 re-runs the whole onboarding save and re-shows the loading + advice overlay
findings.evidence: void _back() { if (_step <= 0) return; ... setState(() => _step--); }  // index 22 → 21 rebuilds _Step18 → initState → _start() → _runSave()
findings.detail: _back() decrements unconditionally. From Step 19 ('Your week') the previous index is _Step18, whose initState immediately re-fires completeOnboarding — duplicate PATCH /me/profile + /me/settings writes, potentially a second AI plan call (prewarm already consumed at line 476-477), and the GoalAdviceRevealOverlay pushed again. The user just wanted to re-read the previous screen.
findings.fix: Skip index 21 when navigating backwards (jump 22 → 20), or make _Step18 a no-op replay when _p._synced is already true.

findings.title: Step 18 retry leaks the smoothing Timer — progress animates faster after every retry
findings.evidence: _smooth = Timer.periodic(const Duration(milliseconds: 33), (t) { ... });  // _start() never cancels the previous timer; retry at line 3092 calls _start() again
findings.detail: 'TAP TO RETRY' calls _start(), which overwrites _smooth with a new Timer.periodic without cancelling the old one. Both timers keep ticking (each adds +0.012 per 33ms), so after N retries progress fills N+1x too fast, and dispose() only cancels the last timer — the rest leak until the widget unmounts.
findings.fix: Call _smooth?.cancel() at the top of _start().

findings.title: Step 19 presents a hardcoded MON-SUN plan as 'This is your week' while the real AI plan is discarded
findings.evidence: static const _days = [('MON', 'Push Day', ...), ('TUE', 'Easy Run', ...), ...] ... 'This is your week, $firstName.'
findings.detail: _Step18 actually generates a real plan (result.plan) but only goalAdvice is read from it (line 3036); Step 19 then shows the same static 7-day template to every user ('Adapts as you train') regardless of their cadence answer (a '2-3 days' user is shown 6 training days) or what the AI generated. The first thing shown after 'Plan generated.' is not their plan.
findings.fix: Pass result.plan into _Step19 and render the actual generated week; fall back to an honest 'your plan is waiting in the Train tab' state if the plan call failed.

findings.title: '+ FOLLOW' social cards never open Instagram/TikTok/YouTube/X — they only flip a local flag
findings.evidence: void toggle(String id) { set(() { if (followed.contains(id)) followed.remove(id); else followed.add(id); }); }
findings.detail: Tapping a platform card marks it 'followed' (check badge, CTA becomes 'Continue · N followed') and the list is sent to the backend as followedSocial, but no url_launcher call ever opens @zveltapp. The user believes they followed the accounts; nothing happened. The recorded analytics data is also false.
findings.fix: Launch the platform profile URL on tap and mark followed only after returning, or relabel the interaction (e.g. 'remind me later').

findings.title: Step 22 claims the progress photo is 'ENCRYPTED · ONLY YOU SEE IT' but files are stored unencrypted
findings.evidence: Text('ENCRYPTED · ONLY YOU SEE IT', ...)  // vs photo_progress_service.dart:40-42: "For v1.1 we plan AES-256 at-rest encryption ... deferred so we don't add deps now."
findings.detail: PhotoProgressService saves the JPEG in the plain app documents sandbox (photo_progress_service.dart:78-96); only the metadata DB is SQLCipher-encrypted. The onboarding UI makes an explicit, currently-false encryption claim about sensitive body photos.
findings.fix: Change the copy to 'PRIVATE · STAYS ON THIS DEVICE' until at-rest encryption ships.

findings.title: Romanian developer instructions shown to end users on connection failure
findings.evidence: 'Nu mă pot conecta la server ($apiBaseUrl). Pornește backend-ul pe PC. ... flutter run --dart-define=API_BASE_URL=http://192.168.x.x:3000'
findings.detail: _authNetworkHint() is thrown for any network failure in login/signup/Google sign-in and rendered verbatim in LoginScreen's error box (login_screen.dart:68-69); authErrorToEnglish has no rule matching it, so a production user with bad Wi-Fi sees Romanian dev instructions including a flutter run command.
findings.fix: Gate the dev hint behind kDebugMode and show a plain 'Can't reach the server — check your connection' message in release builds.

findings.title: No 'Forgot password' path anywhere in the auth flow
findings.evidence: children: [ FilledButton(...sign in...), ZveltSecondaryButton(...Google...), TextButton(...No account?...) ]
findings.detail: LoginScreen offers sign in, sign up and Google only. A user who forgot their password hits a hard dead end ('Invalid email or password.' forever) with no recovery, reset, or support link.
findings.fix: Add a 'Forgot password?' link wired to a reset endpoint, or at minimum a support contact.

findings.title: Body-stats defaults (178cm / 78kg / 28yr) are saved as the user's real profile if sliders are never touched
findings.evidence: double height = 178; double weight = 78; double age = 28; ... footer: _OnbCta(label: 'Continue', onTap: onNext, disabled: p.sex == null)
findings.detail: Step 13's Continue is gated only on sex. Height/weight/age defaults flow straight into PATCH /me/profile (bodyweightKg etc.), nutrition targets and the AI plan, and Step 22 even displays '${widget.p.weight.round()}kg · You' (line 3644) — numbers the user may never have entered or seen as defaults.
findings.fix: Track 'touched' state per slider, or start the sliders unset and require interaction like sex.

findings.title: PR inputs have no upper bound — '9999' kg bench is accepted and synced
findings.evidence: double? _pr(String raw, {required bool unknown}) { if (unknown) return null; final v = double.tryParse(...); if (v == null || v <= 0) return null; return v; }
findings.detail: Step 15 free-text PR fields are only filtered for <=0/NaN; the project spec mandates weight_kg 0-500. Implausible values silently feed the backend settings payload and AI plan generation with no inline validation feedback.
findings.fix: Clamp/flag values outside 1-500kg with an inline error before allowing Continue.

findings.title: Dead state: _Profile.signInMethod is never assigned and _StepAITalk._done is never set true
findings.evidence: String? signInMethod;  // never written anywhere (grep: only declared/sent) ... bool _done = false; // line 2769, only read at 2823/2844
findings.detail: signInMethod is always null since _StepSignIn was removed, yet it's still serialized into the settings payload (onboarding_service.dart:405). _StepAITalk._done is never set, so its 'useCheck: _done' and the _done branch of the CTA label/commit logic are unreachable; after submitting a goal and back-navigating, the CTA misleadingly reads 'Skip for now' even though a vision was captured.
findings.fix: Set signInMethod from the actual auth path (or drop the field), and set _done = true after _submitGoal succeeds.

findings.title: Feature-tour steps show specific unlabeled mock data (e1RM 87.5kg, 1,847 kcal macros, VO2 47, HRV 62)
findings.detail: Steps 05/06/09 and _StepBody present believable product screenshots with concrete numbers and zero 'example/preview' labeling. As feature demos they are more defensible than the user-specific claims in Steps 20/21, but given the owner's hard rule on fabricated data they should be visibly marked as illustrative.
findings.fix: Add a small 'PREVIEW' / 'EXAMPLE DATA' tag on the mock cards.

findings.title: Offline-logged meals silently overwritten (lost) by server sync
findings.evidence: syncHistoryFromServer: `final daily = _dailyFromServerJson(raw); await _saveDayToPrefs(dayDate, daily);` — unconditional overwrite of local prefs, no merge. getDay (line 799-804) only rescues local data when the remote day is COMPLETELY empty: `if (remoteEmpty && localHas) { daily = local; ... } else { await _saveDayToPrefs(date, daily); }`
findings.detail: Scenario: user logs breakfast online (synced), then logs lunch offline — addEntry saves to prefs but _pushDayToServer fails silently (caught, only reportError). On reconnect, _load() first calls syncHistoryFromServer(days:14) which blindly overwrites today's local prefs with the server copy (which lacks lunch). Even without that, getDay's merge guard only fires when remote is fully empty; a partially-filled remote day (breakfast present) overwrites local, deleting the offline lunch entry. addEntry also calls getDay first (line 834), so the next add bakes the loss in permanently and pushes it back to the server. This directly violates the offline-first principle for the nutrition tracker and loses user-entered data with no error shown.
findings.fix: Merge by entry id instead of whole-day overwrite: union local+remote entries (dedupe on entry.id, keep newer waterMl/weightKg by an updatedAt stamp), or keep a dirty/unsynced flag per day and never overwrite a dirty local day until its push succeeds.

findings.title: XP claim card is live in the UI but the feature is a stub — always awards +0 XP and falsely says 'Already claimed'
findings.evidence: `Future<NutritionXpClaimResult> claimDayXp() async { return const NutritionXpClaimResult(xpAwarded: 0, totalXp: 0, lines: [], alreadyClaimed: true); }` — comment admits: "The feature is currently inert". Yet nutrition_tab.dart:412 still renders `NutritionXpClaimCard(...)` and the card's pre-claim copy promises "Hit your daily macros (within ±10%) to earn XP" (nutrition_xp_claim_card.dart:126).
findings.detail: The card sits prominently in the Nutrition tab, promises XP for hitting macros, and renders a working-looking 'Claim today's XP' button. Tapping it always shows '+0 XP' and 'Already claimed' (nutrition_xp_claim_card.dart:158, 189-193) — which is fabricated: nothing was ever claimed and macros were never evaluated. This is exactly the kind of fake data/dead-end feature the owner flagged as non-negotiable.
findings.fix: Hide NutritionXpClaimCard from nutrition_tab.dart until the backend XP endpoint returns, or replace it with an honest 'XP rewards coming back soon' note.

findings.title: Notification badge dot hardcoded to always-on
findings.evidence: `_NutritionHeaderBtn(icon: Icons.notifications_none_rounded, hasDot: true, onTap: ...)` — `hasDot: true` is a literal, not driven by any unread state.
findings.detail: The nutrition header always shows an unread-notification dot regardless of whether the user has notifications. This fabricates an 'unread' signal to drive taps and trains users to ignore the badge.
findings.fix: Drive hasDot from the real unread count (e.g. SocialNotificationHub / notifications service), defaulting to false.

findings.title: _today captured once at State creation — tab logs to the wrong day after midnight
findings.evidence: `final DateTime _today = DateTime.now();` — used by _load (getDay(_today)), _showWaterDialog (updateWater(result, _today)), _showWeightDialog and removeEntry, while new MealEntry uses `loggedAt: DateTime.now()` (line 2006).
findings.detail: NutritionTab lives inside the persistent bottom-nav shell, so its State survives across days. After midnight: water/weight/entry-removal write to yesterday's key, while a newly added food (loggedAt = real now) is saved under the new day but the screen still displays yesterday — the added entry appears to vanish.
findings.fix: Compute today inside _load()/each action (`final today = DateTime.now();`) instead of caching it in a final field, or refresh _today on app resume / tab focus.

findings.title: AI weekly-plan generation hijacks the whole tab with a bare spinner for 30–150s, auto-triggered without consent
findings.evidence: `if (mounted) setState(() => _loading = true);` in _bootstrapWeekPlan (same in _regenerateWeeklyPlanWithAi line 236) replaces the entire tab body with `Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator(...))` (line 371-372). Auto-fired on first open: `if (mounted && _signedIn && _weekPlan.isEmpty && !_autoBootstrapTried) { ... unawaited(_bootstrapWeekPlan()); }` (lines 150-153). Timeout is kNutritionWeeklyPlanGenerateTimeout = 150s.
findings.detail: While the AI plan generates (acknowledged 30-120s in the comments), the user cannot log food, water, or weight — the core tracker is fully blocked behind an unlabelled spinner. Worse, it auto-starts the first time the tab opens empty (e.g. right after onboarding), so a new user's first impression of Nutrition can be up to 2.5 minutes of spinner with no explanation or cancel.
findings.fix: Generate the plan in the background and show an in-card progress state on _WeeklyPlanCard ('Generating your plan… ~1-2 min') while keeping the rest of the tab interactive.

findings.title: Food logging is gated on a 45s-timeout network round-trip (addEntry → getDay)
findings.evidence: `Future<void> addEntry(MealEntry entry) async { ... final day = await getDay(entry.loggedAt); ...}` — getDay does `await http.get(Uri.parse('$v1Base/nutrition/day?date=$q'), ...).withTimeout();` where kHttpTimeout = 45s (http_client.dart:11). updateWater/updateWeight/removeEntry do the same.
findings.detail: On a flaky connection (request hangs rather than failing fast), the 'Add to diary' button spinner (_PortionSheet _adding) can block for up to 45 seconds before the entry is persisted, because every local write first round-trips the server. For an offline-first tracker, a local write should never wait on the network.
findings.fix: In addEntry/updateWater/updateWeight/removeEntry, read the day from local prefs (_loadDayFromPrefsOnly), write locally, return, and push to the server fire-and-forget (already unawaited-safe via _pushDayToServer).

findings.title: No validation on goals/weight inputs — calorie goal of 0 produces NaN progress; weight accepts any value despite 30–250 kg spec
findings.evidence: _CaloriesCard: `final progress = (consumed / goal).clamp(0.0, 1.0);` — no goal>0 guard (unlike _MacroCard line 1003 and _WaterCard line 1070 which guard). _GoalsSheet saves `calories: int.tryParse(_calCtrl.text) ?? 2000` (line 2594) so typing '0' is accepted. Weight dialog pops `double.tryParse(ctrl.text.replaceAll(',', '.'))` (line 348) with no bounds before `_service.updateWeight(result, _today)`.
findings.detail: With a saved calorie goal of 0 and 0 consumed, 0/0 = NaN, and LinearProgressIndicator(value: NaN) hits a paint assert in debug and renders garbage in release. Weight log accepts 0.5 or 9999 kg with no warning, contradicting the project's mandatory `bodyweight_kg 30–250` validation rule; the bogus value then syncs to the server and shows in the Weight card. Also, clearing a goal field silently saves the hardcoded default (e.g. 2000) instead of warning.
findings.fix: Guard `goal > 0` in _CaloriesCard like the other cards; clamp/validate goal fields (e.g. calories 800–10000) and weight (30–250 kg) before saving, showing inline errors.

findings.title: Goals edited offline are silently reverted by the server profile on next load
findings.evidence: getGoals: `final fromServer = await _fetchGoalsFromProfile(); if (fromServer != null) { ... await prefs.setString(await _goalsKey(), jsonEncode(fromServer.toJson())); return fromServer; }` while saveGoals swallows PATCH failures: `catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'nutrition:save-goals-patch'); }` (lines 972-974).
findings.detail: If the PATCH /me/profile fails (offline, cold dyno timeout), saveGoals reports success to the UI ('goals saved' locally) but the next _load() fetches the old server goals and overwrites the local copy — the user's edit silently disappears with no error.
findings.fix: Track a local dirty flag for goals; prefer the local copy until a PATCH succeeds, or surface the PATCH failure to the user.

findings.title: Barcode lookup falls back to an arbitrary single search result, can log the wrong product
findings.evidence: `if (match == null) { if (foods.length == 1 && foods.first is Map) { match = Map<String, dynamic>.from(foods.first as Map); } else { return null; } }` plus loose GTIN equivalence at line 259: `return da.endsWith(db) || db.endsWith(da);`
findings.detail: lookupBrandedByGtin runs a free-text search on the barcode digits; if no result's gtinUpc matches but exactly one result comes back, it is accepted as 'the' product without any GTIN verification — the user then logs macros for a food they didn't scan, presented as a confident match. The suffix-based _gtinEquivalent can also false-positive (e.g. an 8-digit code matching the tail of an unrelated 13-digit GTIN).
findings.fix: Drop the single-result fallback (return null → existing 'Product not found' snackbar), or show the candidate with an explicit 'closest match — verify' label. Restrict _gtinEquivalent suffix matching to the documented UPC-A↔EAN-13 zero-padding case.

findings.title: Stale search results race: clearing the query below 3 chars doesn't invalidate in-flight searches
findings.evidence: onChanged: `if (t.length < 3) { if (mounted) { setState(() { _results = []; _loading = false; _searchError = null; }); } return; }` — clears UI but does not bump `_searchGeneration`, so a previously-fired `_search` (guard at line 1396: `if (!mounted || gen != _searchGeneration) return;`) still passes the generation check and repopulates results.
findings.detail: Type 'chicken' (search fires), quickly delete to 'ch' — results clear, then the in-flight 'chicken' response lands and fills the list while the field shows 'ch'. Results no longer correspond to the visible query.
findings.fix: Increment `_searchGeneration` (`++_searchGeneration;`) in the <3-chars clearing branch (both in onChanged and in _search's early return).

findings.title: 'No results' shown during the debounce window before any search has run
findings.evidence: `: _results.isEmpty && _searchCtrl.text.trim().length >= 3 && _searchError == null ? Center(child: Text('No results', ...))` — `_loading` only flips true when _search actually executes after the 500ms debounce.
findings.detail: While the user is still typing the 3rd+ character, the empty-results branch immediately shows 'No results' for ~500ms before the debounced search fires, implying the database has nothing for a query that hasn't been searched yet.
findings.fix: Track a `_hasSearched` flag (or treat the pending debounce timer as loading) and only show 'No results' after a completed search for the current query.

findings.title: Header icon buttons are 40×40 with no semantics — below the 44pt minimum, invisible to screen readers
findings.evidence: _NutritionHeaderBtn: `GestureDetector(onTap: onTap, child: ... Container(width: 40, height: 40, ...))` — raw GestureDetector, no Semantics/tooltip. Likewise 'Edit goals' is a bare `GestureDetector(onTap: ..., child: Text('Edit goals', ... fontSize: 12))` (lines 425-435) with a tap target of roughly 12pt height.
findings.detail: The notifications and goals header buttons fail the project's >=44pt touch-target rule, and as bare GestureDetectors they expose no semantic label or button role to TalkBack/VoiceOver. The 'Edit goals' text link is an even smaller target.
findings.fix: Use IconButton (default 48dp hit area, tooltip = semantics) or wrap in Semantics(button:true, label:) with a 44pt min constraint; make 'Edit goals' a TextButton.

findings.title: Journal: missed past days can never be backfilled
findings.evidence: `Future<void> _openComposer({JournalEntry? existing, DateTime? date}) async { ... final target = date ?? existing?.entryDate ?? DateTime(now.year, now.month, now.day);` — the `date` parameter is never passed from any call site (line 147 `_openComposer(existing: today)`, line 213 `_openComposer()`, line 342 `_openComposer(existing: e)`).
findings.detail: The composer fully supports arbitrary dates (JournalEntryScreen takes a date and titles itself accordingly), but the UI offers no path to log yesterday — the 30-day trend chart shows permanent gaps the user can see but never fill. Mildly frustrating for the exact 'forgot to log last night' case a wellness journal invites.
findings.fix: Make gaps in the 30-day chart (or a small date picker next to the + button) tappable, calling `_openComposer(date: thatDay)`.

findings.title: Dead service methods: getByCategory / getCategories always return empty and have no callers
findings.evidence: `Future<List<FoodItem>> getByCategory(String category) async => [];` and `Future<List<String>> getCategories() async => [];` — grep across lib/ shows no call sites.
findings.detail: Leftover stubs from a previous food-database design. Harmless but they look like working API surface to the next developer.
findings.fix: Delete both methods.

findings.title: Error UI uses the cardio activity color instead of the error token
findings.evidence: _showPersistentError: `backgroundColor: ZveltTokens.cardio` (and the entry swipe-delete background at line 1312: `color: ZveltTokens.cardio.withValues(alpha: 0.12)`, error banner at 1533) — tokens define `error = Color(0xFFE5484D)` while `cardio = Color(0xFFFF6B6B)` is the cardio-activity accent.
findings.detail: Error/destructive affordances in the nutrition area are tinted with the cardio activity color rather than ZveltTokens.error, so 'error red' here differs from error red elsewhere (journal delete correctly uses ZveltTokens.error). Semantic token misuse that will bite any future theme change.
findings.fix: Replace ZveltTokens.cardio with ZveltTokens.error for error snackbars, the dismiss-to-delete background, and the search error banner.

findings.title: Units↔grams toggle clamps unit count to 80 while the slider max is 24 — value and thumb disagree
findings.evidence: _syncMode: `_unitCount = raw.clamp(0.25, 80);` but the Slider renders `value: ... _unitCount.clamp(0.25, 24), min: ... max: 24` (lines 1913-1917).
findings.detail: For small-serving foods (e.g. servingGrams ~5g) switching from 400g grams-mode to units yields _unitCount up to 80; the slider thumb pins at 24 while the macro preview and '<N> g total' label compute from 80 units — the displayed numbers don't match the slider position until the user drags it.
findings.fix: Use the same upper bound (24) in _syncMode's clamp, or raise the slider max to match.

findings.title: 'Add food' bottom CTA silently hardwired to Breakfast
findings.evidence: `onPressed: () => _showAddFood('breakfast'),` on the generic full-width 'Add food' button at the bottom of the tab.
findings.detail: The CTA gives no hint that the food will land in Breakfast; a user adding dinner via this button gets the entry filed under the wrong meal with no way to move it afterwards (no edit/move-entry flow exists — only swipe-to-delete and re-add).
findings.fix: Default to a meal inferred from time of day, or add a meal selector inside _AddFoodSheet / _PortionSheet.

findings.title: Race hub never initializes joined state — server 'joined' flag ignored, so already-joined races always show 'JOIN THE RACE' and re-joining inflates the athlete count
findings.evidence: final joined = _joinedChallenges[challenge.id] ?? false;  // map is only ever written by _toggleJoin, never seeded
findings.detail: The SocialChallenge model parses `joined` and `participantsCount` from the server (social_challenge.dart:144-145, doc comment: "True when the viewer is in the participant set — drives Join/Leave label"), but a grep shows `.joined` is never read by any screen. RaceHubScreen seeds `_joinedChallenges` only from in-session taps. Consequences: (1) after app restart or arriving via the feed hero (which DID join via _joinAndOpenRace), every joined race still shows 'JOIN THE RACE'; (2) tapping it calls joinChallenge again (server returns 200 'already joined', treated as success at social_challenge_service.dart:192) and then `_participantCounts[challengeId] = (count ?? 0) + 1` (line 281) increments a count the user was already part of — a fabricated participant number on screen; (3) a user who joined in a previous session cannot leave without first 'joining' again.
findings.fix: Seed `_joinedChallenges[c.id] = c.joined` and `_participantCounts[c.id] = c.participantsCount` in _loadChallenges, and reconcile counts from the participants endpoint instead of local +1/-1 deltas.

findings.title: Race Notes labeled 'PRIVATE · ONLY YOU' but every note body is POSTed to the backend
findings.evidence: unawaited(_postToServer(challengeId, trimmed));  // POST $v1Base/challenges/$challengeId/chat/messages
findings.detail: The Race Notes UI promises locality three times: the 'PRIVATE · ONLY YOU' pill (race_hub_screen.dart:850), hint 'Drop a note (only you see this)…' (line 1021), and empty state 'Only you see this.' (line 1121). Yet sendMessage fire-and-forgets the full note body to `/v1/challenges/{id}/chat/messages`. Today the endpoint 404s, but the content still leaves the device on every send, and the file header (line 4) says the plan is to 'flip to server-of-record' — meaning the moment the v1.1 endpoint ships, all 'private' notes silently start landing in a multi-athlete chat room. This directly contradicts the privacy-by-default principle and the on-screen promise.
findings.fix: Remove the _postToServer call while the UI claims privacy; reintroduce it only behind an explicit user-facing 'send to race chat' action in v1.1.

findings.title: User profile stats card shows fabricated zeros when the backend omits stats
findings.evidence: ('Sessions', s?.sessions ?? 0), ('PRs', s?.prs ?? 0), ('Streak', s?.streak ?? 0),
findings.detail: The model deliberately keeps these nullable ('null until then so the UI shows honest zeros', line 606-607), but the render collapses null → 0. A veteran user with 300 sessions whose profile endpoint doesn't yet include a `stats` block renders as 'Sessions 0 · PRs 0 · Streak 0' — indistinguishable from a real new account. That is fabricated data presented as real, the exact thing principle #1 forbids. The comment at line 471 ('Shows honest zeros for a new account; fills in once the backend includes these') admits the backend may not send them yet, which means today every profile shows 0/0/0.
findings.fix: When sessions/prs/streak are null, render '—' (or hide the card entirely) instead of 0; only show numerals when the backend actually sent them.

findings.title: Incoming friend requests can only be accepted — there is no decline/reject action
findings.evidence: FilledButton(onPressed: () => _accept(r), child: const Text('Accept')),  // only action on incoming rows
findings.detail: The Requests tab renders incoming rows with a single Accept button. The service already has `removeOrCancel(userId)` (friends_service.dart:182) used for outgoing cancellation, so a Decline is one call away. As shipped, an unwanted request from a stranger sits in the list forever (it also inflates the 'Requests (N)' tab badge at line 366); the only escape hatches are accepting the person or finding them elsewhere to block. For a privacy-by-default product this is a notable dead end.
findings.fix: Add a 'Decline' TextButton next to Accept that calls _service.removeOrCancel(r.userId) and reloads.

findings.title: 'TOP STREAKS' section shows the first 3 friends in list order, not the top streaks
findings.evidence: List<_StreakUser> get _topStreaks => _filteredFriends.take(3).map((f) { ... days: resolved ? _friendStreaks[f.userId] : null,
findings.detail: The header reads '🔥 TOP STREAKS' (line 337) under brand-gradient flame badges, but the three avatars are simply `_filteredFriends.take(3)` — whatever order /v1/friends returned. Even after `_friendStreaks` loads with real values, the selection is never re-sorted, so a friend with a 0-day streak can sit in 'TOP STREAKS' while the actual leader is hidden. The data shown (each badge number) is real, but the claim of the heading is false.
findings.fix: After streaks resolve, sort friends by `_friendStreaks[userId] ?? -1` descending before take(3); until resolved, either keep the dim '—' badges or retitle the loading state.

findings.title: 'RECENTLY ACTIVE' section is just the first 5 friends — no activity/recency data behind it
findings.evidence: List<_RecentUser> get _recent => _filteredFriends.take(5).map((f) { return _RecentUser(... sub: 'IN YOUR CIRCLE', ... online: false,
findings.detail: The section header 'RECENTLY ACTIVE' (line 362) sits next to a green presence-style pill ('N IN CIRCLE' with a success-colored dot, lines 371-399), implying live/recent activity. In reality the rows are an arbitrary take(5) of the friends list with a hardcoded subtitle and `online: false`. No last-active timestamp exists anywhere in FriendSummary. The heading promises a signal the app does not have. Additionally, with >5 friends there is no way to see the rest on this screen (no 'view all' under the list — only the small header icon routes to FriendsScreen).
findings.fix: Rename the section to 'YOUR CIRCLE', drop the green presence pill styling (or change to neutral), and add a 'See all (N)' row when _friends.length > 5.

findings.title: Deep-link snackbar claims "You're in: <race>" even when the join request failed
findings.evidence: try { await _challengeService.joinChallenge(c.id); } catch (e, st) { // Already-joined / network failures are non-fatal ... } ... await _openRaceHub(initialChallengeId: c.id);
findings.detail: _joinAndOpenRace swallows ALL join failures (including network drop and 4xx/5xx) and still opens RaceHubScreen with initialChallengeId, where _maybeOpenDeepLink unconditionally shows `Text("You're in: ${matched.title}")` (race_hub_screen.dart:160). On a failed join the user is told they joined a race they did not join — and because the hub also never seeds joined state (see separate finding), nothing on screen contradicts the false confirmation.
findings.fix: Propagate join success/failure from _joinAndOpenRace (e.g. pass `joined: bool` to RaceHubScreen) and only show the "You're in" snackbar when the join actually succeeded; show a retryable error otherwise.

findings.title: Race hub load failure silently renders the 'No active races — Be the first to launch one!' empty state
findings.evidence: } catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'race-hub:load-challenges'); }  // no error state set
findings.detail: _loadChallenges has no error UI path; combined with SocialChallengeService.loadActive falling back to local on ANY non-200 (social_challenge_service.dart:52-62), a 401/500 or offline launch shows the trending row's empty state 'No active races / Be the first to launch one!' (lines 468-474). The user can't distinguish 'there really are no races' from 'the request failed' and has no visible retry (Refresh is buried in the settings sheet).
findings.fix: Track an _error flag when loadActive falls back due to failure (have the service surface it) and render a ZveltErrorState with onRetry, matching DirectChatScreen and UserProfileScreen.

findings.title: Circle friends load failure shows the 'No recent activity — Add friends' empty state with no error or retry
findings.evidence: } catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'circle:load-friends'); if (!mounted) return; setState(() => _loadingFriends = false); }
findings.detail: When listFriends throws (it throws on any non-200, friends_service.dart:64), CircleScreen just flips the spinner off, leaving `_friends = []`. The screen then renders '0 IN CIRCLE' and the empty state 'No recent activity / Add friends to see their activity here.' — telling a user with 20 friends that they have none. No retry affordance exists anywhere on the screen (no pull-to-refresh either; the ListView has no RefreshIndicator).
findings.fix: Add an _error state rendering ZveltErrorState with onRetry: _loadFriends, and wrap the ListView in a RefreshIndicator.

findings.title: FriendsService has no timeouts on any HTTP call — friends screens can spin indefinitely
findings.evidence: final res = await http.get(Uri.parse('$v1Base/friends'), headers: await _headers());  // no .timeout()/.withTimeout()
findings.detail: Every other service in this area guards requests (MessagesService uses .withTimeout(), SocialChallengeService uses .timeout(22s)), but all 7 calls in FriendsService (listFriends, searchByUsername, incoming/outgoingRequests, sendRequest, acceptRequest, removeOrCancel) have none. A stalled connection leaves FriendsScreen on its full-screen spinner (_loading never resolves) and leaves CircleScreen loading forever, with no way for the user to recover other than killing the screen.
findings.fix: Apply the shared withTimeout() extension from http_client.dart to every request in FriendsService.

findings.title: incomingRequests/outgoingRequests swallow non-200 into empty lists — server errors render as 'No pending requests'
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode != 200) return [];  // same at line 136 for outgoing and line 88 for search
findings.detail: If /friends succeeds but /friends/requests/incoming returns 500, FriendsScreen._reload completes 'successfully' and the Requests tab shows 'No pending requests' (friends_screen.dart:588) — a real pending request from a friend silently disappears from the UI with no error. Same pattern for outgoing requests and username search. This both masks failures and shows false data ('no requests' when the truth is unknown).
findings.fix: Throw on non-200 like listFriends does, letting FriendsScreen._reload surface its existing ZveltErrorState, or return a nullable result the UI can render as an inline error row.

findings.title: DM push-driven silent reload throws away paged-in history and yanks scroll to the bottom
findings.evidence: setState(() { _msgs = list; // Re-anchor pagination state — a silent reload always replaces the currently-visible window ... }); WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback((_) => _scrollToEnd());
findings.detail: _silentReload (triggered by any FCM bump on RefreshScope.dm, throttled to 2s) replaces _msgs with only the latest page and then unconditionally jumps to maxScrollExtent. If the user tapped 'Load earlier' a few times and is reading week-old messages, one incoming push (even in another conversation if the scope is global) wipes the prepended history and teleports them to the newest message, losing their place. The 'changed' guard (lines 100-103) doesn't help: prepended history makes the lengths differ, so it always counts as changed.
findings.fix: On silent reload, append only messages newer than _msgs.last.id instead of replacing the list, and only auto-scroll when the user is already at/near the bottom.

findings.title: Friends list: long-press = Block (hidden destructive gesture) and a plain tap does nothing — no path to the friend's profile
findings.evidence: return GestureDetector( onLongPress: () => _blockFriend(u), child: _UserTile(
findings.detail: Long-pressing a friend row — a gesture users commonly try for context menus or reordering — immediately opens the 'Block this user?' dialog. Block is already discoverably available as an explicit trailing IconButton (line 425), so the hidden gesture adds only accident risk. Meanwhile a normal tap on the row is dead: there is no navigation to UserProfileScreen from either the friends list or the search results, so you can't view someone's profile/posts before adding or messaging them, even though the screen exists and is reachable from feed posts.
findings.fix: Replace onLongPress-block with onTap → UserProfileScreen(userId: u.userId, displayName: u.displayName, username: u.username); keep block only as the explicit button.

findings.title: Touch targets well below the 44pt minimum across race hub and circle
findings.evidence: child: AnimatedContainer( duration: const Duration(milliseconds: 200), height: 28,  // JOIN THE RACE / LEAVE RACE tap area
findings.detail: The product principle requires >=44pt targets, but: the JOIN/LEAVE pill on trending cards is 28px tall (race_hub_screen.dart:538); the QUICK NOTES pills are 30px (line 967); the emoji-picker trigger is a bare 18px icon in a GestureDetector (lines 1030-1035); and CircleScreen's chat button _SmallCircleBtn is 34x34 (circle_screen.dart:798-799). All are primary interactions (joining a race, opening a chat). Additionally none of these GestureDetector-based controls (including _DiscCard, _PresetPill, _PrivacyOption) carry Semantics(button:), so screen readers announce them as plain text — in contrast to UserProfileScreen which wraps its actions in Semantics correctly.
findings.fix: Give each control a minimum 44x44 hit area (e.g. ConstrainedBox/behavior: opaque with padding) and wrap in Semantics(button: true, label: ...) or switch to InkWell/IconButton.

findings.title: 'EXPLORE ALL' in the race hub looks like a button but is a dead, non-tappable label
findings.evidence: Text('EXPLORE ALL', style: TextStyle(... color: ZveltTokens.brand, letterSpacing: 1.5)),  // no GestureDetector/InkWell
findings.detail: It is styled exactly like an action (brand color, all-caps, paired against the 'GLOBAL TRENDING' section header) but has no tap handler and no destination. Given commit 317f023 ('fix dead/stub buttons found by the multi-agent audit') this class of issue is actively being hunted; this one survived.
findings.fix: Either remove the label or wire it to a full races list; if no destination exists in v1.0, remove it rather than ship a decoy.

findings.title: RaceChatService.clearForChallenge is never called — 'wipe on leave' documented but not wired, race notes persist after leaving
findings.evidence: /// Wipe history for a single race — called when the user leaves a race. Future<void> clearForChallenge(String challengeId) async {
findings.detail: A project-wide grep finds zero call sites outside the definition. RaceHubScreen._toggleJoin's leave path (race_hub_screen.dart:271-276) does not invoke it, so notes written for a race remain in the encrypted local DB indefinitely after leaving. Low impact since the store is local and encrypted, but the doc comment describes behavior that doesn't exist.
findings.fix: Call RaceChatService().clearForChallenge(challengeId) on successful leave (ideally after a confirm, since notes are user content), or update the doc comment.

findings.title: Hardcoded 'Race launched · 0 athletes joined' snackbar asserts a count the client never checked
findings.evidence: content: Text('Race launched · 0 athletes joined'),
findings.detail: The count is a string literal, not data. It happens to be plausibly true at launch time, but if the backend ever auto-joins the creator (common for challenges) it becomes false, and it sets the pattern of asserting numbers the client didn't measure.
findings.fix: Drop the count ('Race launched') or read the actual participantsCount from the freshly reloaded challenge.

findings.title: Two different hardcoded 'brand' gradients and non-token greys within the same area
findings.evidence: colors: [Color(0xFFFF5A1F), Color(0xFFD9360B)],  // vs race_hub_screen.dart:328 [Color(0xFFFF3D1F), Color(0xFFFF7A2F)]
findings.detail: CircleScreen's brand gradient (FF5A1F→D9360B) differs from RaceHubScreen's (FF3D1F→FF7A2F) for visually identical circular action buttons, and CircleScreen also hardcodes secondary-text grey Color(0xFF6B7280) (lines 286, 294, 341, 366, 449, 756) and divider Color(0xFF1A2030) (line 730) instead of ZveltTokens.text2/border. None of these exist in zvelt_tokens.dart, so future theme changes will fork these screens. (Related cosmetic dead code: _StreakAvatar renders an invisible 3x3 gradient dot behind the 64px avatar, circle_screen.dart:627-643.)
findings.fix: Promote one brand gradient to ZveltTokens (e.g. ZveltTokens.brandGradient) and replace the hardcoded greys with text2/border tokens.

findings.title: Participant counts fetched serially — one awaited HTTP round-trip per challenge
findings.evidence: for (final c in _challenges) { try { final result = await _challengeService.getChallengeParticipants(c.id);
findings.detail: _loadParticipantCounts awaits each /challenges/{id}/participants call (22s timeout each) in sequence. With 10 active races on a slow connection the trending row's counts trickle in over many seconds, and a worst case approaches minutes. Note the feed response already carries participantsCount (social_challenge.dart:144) which would make most of these calls unnecessary.
findings.fix: Seed counts from challenge.participantsCount and, if a refresh is still wanted, use Future.wait over the per-challenge fetches.

findings.title: User profile posts errors are masked as the 'No posts yet' empty state
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode < 200 || res.statusCode >= 300) { setState(() { _posts = []; _loadingPosts = false; }); return; }
findings.detail: Any non-2xx (and any thrown exception, line 181-184) from /users/:id/posts renders the same compact 'No posts yet' empty state (line 390-397) used for a genuinely empty profile. A user whose friend's posts fail to load is told the friend has never posted — false information with no retry except the undiscoverable pull-to-refresh. The profile half of this screen handles errors carefully (404/403/network/timeout each get distinct states), which makes the posts half's silence inconsistent.
findings.fix: Track a _postsError flag and render a compact ZveltErrorState with an inline retry instead of the empty state.

findings.title: Delete account screen renders white text on a light background — flow is unreadable
findings.evidence: style: const TextStyle(color: Colors.white, fontWeight: FontWeight.w800, ...) — while the Scaffold uses backgroundColor: ZveltTokens.bg (line 106), which is the V2 LIGHT token #F6F7F5 (zvelt_tokens.dart:21)
findings.detail: The entire screen was authored for the old dark theme and never migrated to the V2 light token system. The AppBar title (line 116), back chevron (line 111), the 'This is permanent' warning (line 139), every 'What gets deleted' item title (line 178), the timeline rows (line 312), the 'Type DELETE to confirm' header (line 216) and — worst — the confirmation TextField input text (line 225) are all hardcoded Colors.white, drawn over ZveltTokens.bg #F6F7F5 or ZveltTokens.surface #FFFFFF. Contrast is ~1.05:1, i.e. invisible. The user cannot read the deletion warnings, cannot see the instruction to type DELETE, and cannot see what they type. This is the Play-policy-mandated account deletion path, effectively broken visually.
findings.fix: Replace all hardcoded Colors.white in this screen with ZveltTokens.text (and ZveltTokens.text2 for secondary copy); keep Colors.white only inside the red CTA button.

findings.title: Appearance setting (Dark/Light/Auto) persists but does nothing — both themes resolve to the same light ThemeData
findings.evidence: static ThemeData get lightThemeData => themeData;  static ThemeData get darkThemeData => themeData; — and main.dart:275-277 wires theme: AppTheme.lightThemeData, darkTheme: AppTheme.themeData, themeMode: themeMode
findings.detail: Settings exposes a three-way Appearance selector (settings_screen.dart:1325-1344) backed by ZveltThemeNotifier, which dutifully persists the mode to SharedPreferences. But since the V2 migration, lightThemeData and darkThemeData are the SAME light theme, and every V2 screen hardcodes const light ZveltTokens anyway. Selecting 'Dark' changes nothing visually — a settings toggle that promises a feature that does not exist. Default mode is even ThemeMode.dark (zvelt_theme_notifier.dart:11) while the app renders light.
findings.fix: Either remove the Appearance tile until a real dark palette exists, or implement a dark ThemeData + theme-aware tokens. Don't ship a selector with zero effect.

findings.title: Language setting is written to prefs but nothing in the app reads it — 'restart the app to apply' is a false promise
findings.evidence: _snack('Language updated — restart the app to apply'); — grep for 'zvelt_language' across lib/ matches only settings_screen.dart itself (lines 27, 239, 708); there are no localizationsDelegates/supportedLocales anywhere in lib/
findings.detail: The Language dialog offers 10 languages (Español, 日本語, العربية, ...) and tells the user a restart will apply the change. The pref key is never consumed by MaterialApp or any l10n layer — l10n/app_strings.dart is static English. Restarting changes nothing; the setting only changes its own subtitle. This is a dead setting masquerading as a working feature.
findings.fix: Remove the Language tile until i18n exists, or reduce it to 'English' with a 'more languages coming' note. Do not promise that a restart applies it.

findings.title: Units setting (kg/lb) is only consumed by the Physical data screen — the rest of the app stays metric, contradicting the setting's own label
findings.evidence: subtitle: _unitSystem == 'metric' ? 'Metric (kg, km)' : 'Imperial (lbs, miles)' — but grep for unitSystem/imperial across lib/ shows no workout, cardio, analytics or social screen reads it; skeleton_profile_tab.dart:213 even hardcodes '${_weightKg!.toStringAsFixed(1)} kg' directly above the Units row showing 'Imperial (lbs, in)'
findings.detail: Switching to Imperial PATCHes unitSystem to the backend and changes the weight/height labels on profile_screen.dart only. Workout logging, weekly effort ('Volume (kg×reps)', skeleton_profile_tab.dart:283), distances ('miles' promised) and the profile tab's own bodyweight subtitle remain kg/km. An imperial user sees 'Imperial (lbs, in)' as their active unit while the row directly above shows their weight in kg.
findings.fix: Introduce a single unit-conversion helper consumed by all weight/distance displays, or scope the setting's label honestly (e.g. 'affects body metrics only') until conversion is wired app-wide.

findings.title: Physical data screen shows hardcoded defaults (75 kg / 175 cm) when the profile load fails, and lets the user save them over real data
findings.evidence: } catch (e) { debugPrint('Physical data load error: $e'); }  — with initial values double _weightKg = 75; double _heightCm = 175; String _sex = 'male'; (lines 27-29)
findings.detail: If GET /v1/me fails (offline, timeout, 5xx) the error is only debugPrinted and the editor renders the placeholder defaults as if they were the user's saved values — no error banner, no retry. A user who taps Save then PATCHes 75 kg / 175 cm / male over their real profile, which silently corrupts bodyweight — the input to every strength ranking (SR = e1RM / bodyweight). This directly violates the 'never show fabricated data' principle.
findings.fix: On load failure show an error state with Retry and disable Save until real values are loaded.

findings.title: Physical data screen spinner never resolves if no access token is available
findings.evidence: final token = await _auth.getAccessToken(); if (token == null) return;  — the early return inside _load() skips line 73 'if (mounted) setState(() => _loading = false);'
findings.detail: When getAccessToken() returns null (expired session + failed refresh, e.g. offline), _load() returns before clearing _loading, so the screen shows an infinite CircularProgressIndicator with no way out except the back button.
findings.fix: Move the _loading=false reset into a finally block (or set an auth-error state) so the early return cannot strand the spinner.

findings.title: Units dialog on the profile tab fires an unchecked, uncaught http.patch — silent failure and potential unhandled exception
findings.evidence: await http.patch(Uri.parse('$v1Base/me/profile'), ... body: jsonEncode({'unitSystem': result})); await _load(); — no try/catch, no status-code check, no timeout, no user feedback
findings.detail: If the PATCH returns 4xx/5xx the failure is silently ignored: _load() re-fetches and the dialog selection simply reverts with zero explanation. If the request throws (airplane mode), the exception escapes the async void method entirely. This also duplicates the Settings units dialog (settings_screen.dart:625) which DOES check the result and snack on failure — two divergent implementations of the same setting.
findings.fix: Route this through the same _patchProfile/snack pattern Settings uses (or delete the duplicate dialog and deep-link to Settings).

findings.title: Same backend field privacyDefault is labelled 'Default post privacy' on the profile tab and 'Profile visibility' in Settings
findings.evidence: title: const Text('Default post privacy'), ... jsonEncode({'privacyDefault': result}) — while settings_screen.dart:556-560 presents the same field as 'Profile visibility' with subtitle 'Anyone can view your profile'
findings.detail: Two live entry points PATCH the identical privacyDefault field but describe it as two different things (who sees your posts vs. who sees your profile). A user could set 'Profile visibility: Private' in Settings and be confused that their 'Default post privacy' silently changed too — or vice versa. For a privacy-by-default product this ambiguity about what the control actually governs is a real risk. The profile-tab version also has no try/catch (network error = unhandled exception) and no error feedback on non-200 (lines 480-494).
findings.fix: Keep one control with one accurate label matching the backend semantics; add error handling if the profile-tab entry point stays.

findings.title: Profile tab _initials getter can throw RangeError (empty display name, or double space in name)
findings.evidence: if (parts.length >= 2) return '${parts[0][0]}${parts[1][0]}'.toUpperCase(); return name.substring(0, name.length.clamp(1, 2)).toUpperCase();
findings.detail: If the backend returns displayName == '' (the ?? 'Athlete' fallback only catches null, line 76), name.substring(0, 0.clamp(1,2)=1) throws RangeError on an empty string. Likewise 'John  Smith' (double space) splits to ['John','','Smith'] and parts[1][0] indexes into an empty string. Either case crashes the entire profile tab build.
findings.fix: Filter empty segments: parts = name.trim().split(RegExp(r'\s+')).where((p) => p.isNotEmpty), and guard name.isEmpty with a fallback initial.

findings.title: 2FA toggle fails silently on non-200 responses
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode == 200 && mounted) { setState(() => _twoFaEnabled = !_twoFaEnabled); _snack(...); } — there is no else branch; only thrown exceptions reach the catch with its error snack
findings.detail: If POST /v1/auth/2fa/enable returns 400/409/500, the user confirmed the dialog, the switch doesn't move, and no message of any kind appears — it looks like the tap was swallowed. The same silent non-200 pattern exists in _syncIntegration (line 339) and _connect (line 320).
findings.fix: Add an else branch that surfaces the backend error message via _snack for each of these handlers.

findings.title: OAuth integration connect (Garmin/Fitbit/Polar/Wahoo) never refreshes status after returning from the browser
findings.evidence: Future<void> _connect(String provider) async { ... if (await canLaunchUrl(url)) await launchUrl(url, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication); } — no WidgetsBindingObserver/didChangeAppLifecycleState and no reload after the flow
findings.detail: After completing OAuth in the external browser and switching back to the app, the integration card still shows 'Not connected' until the user manually leaves and re-enters Settings. The Strava tile has the same issue (_connectStrava, line 379). Dead-end feeling: 'I connected it, why does it say not connected?'
findings.fix: Observe AppLifecycleState.resumed while Settings is on screen and re-run _loadIntegrations()/_loadStravaStatus().

findings.title: Achievements screen has no empty state when the list is empty
findings.evidence: itemCount: _achievements.length, — there is no branch for _achievements.isEmpty; a successful 200 with an empty array renders 'Unlocked 0 / XP earned 0' chips above a blank list
findings.detail: A new user (or a backend returning an empty achievements array) sees two zero chips and a large blank void — no explanation that achievements exist and how to earn the first one. This is the screen most likely visited by fresh users from the profile tab's 'Achievements: 0 earned' row.
findings.fix: Add an empty state with copy like 'Complete your first workout to start earning achievements'.

findings.title: Achievements auth-error CTA says 'Sign in' but only pops to the root route
findings.evidence: if (kind == _LoadError.auth) { Navigator.of(context).popUntil((r) => r.isFirst); return; }
findings.detail: The error card promises 'Sign in' but tapping it just dismisses to the first route (the main shell) without logging the user out or presenting any sign-in UI — the user lands back on the app, still in the broken-auth state, with no path to actually re-authenticate.
findings.fix: Either trigger the real logout/onLogout path so AuthGate shows the login screen, or relabel the CTA to 'Go back'.

findings.title: heatmap_screen.dart and integrations_screen.dart are unreferenced dead code (not in the known-dead list)
findings.evidence: class HeatmapScreen extends StatefulWidget — grep across lib/ finds zero imports/constructions outside the file itself; integrations_screen.dart is imported only by account_settings_screen.dart, which is already flagged dead
findings.detail: HeatmapScreen has no live navigation path at all. IntegrationsScreen is reachable only through the known-dead AccountSettingsScreen, making it transitively dead; its functionality is duplicated by the live integration cards inside settings_screen.dart. Both files will drift from the live implementations (IntegrationsScreen already differs from the Settings integration UI).
findings.fix: Delete both files (and the import in account_settings_screen.dart) or add a TODO marking them retired.

findings.title: Physical data screen uses category colors (recovery/cardio) instead of semantic success/error for snackbars
findings.evidence: backgroundColor: ZveltTokens.recovery, — success snack uses the light-blue 'recovery' biometric color #7BC6FF; the error snack uses ZveltTokens.cardio (line 115) instead of ZveltTokens.error
findings.detail: Settings uses ZveltTokens.success/ZveltTokens.error for the identical pattern (_snack, settings_screen.dart:846). Besides the inconsistency, default white SnackBar text on #7BC6FF is roughly 1.9:1 contrast — well below WCAG. The tokens file explicitly says category palette is 'one focus color per screen — don't mix'.
findings.fix: Use ZveltTokens.success / ZveltTokens.error here.

findings.title: Biological sex segmented selector: <44pt touch targets and no button semantics
findings.evidence: GestureDetector(onTap: () => onChanged(options[i].value), child: AnimatedContainer(... padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 12), ...
findings.detail: Each option is fontSize 13 text + 12px vertical padding, around 41-42px tall — under the 44pt minimum the product spec mandates — and uses a raw GestureDetector, so screen readers get no button role, no selected state, and no label grouping with 'Biological sex'. The delete screen's main CTA (delete_account_screen.dart:255) is also a bare GestureDetector without Semantics.
findings.fix: Wrap options in Semantics(button: true, selected: ...) or use InkWell within a 44px-min constraints box; consider SegmentedButton.

findings.title: Profile hero fabricates tier names ('GOLD MEMBER', 'DIAMOND ELITE') that do not exist in the product tier system
findings.evidence: if (gameLevel >= 6) return 'DIAMOND ELITE'; if (gameLevel >= 5) return 'PLATINUM ELITE'; if (gameLevel >= 4) return 'GOLD MEMBER'; ... return 'IRON MEMBER';
findings.detail: When the backend omits levelName, the UI invents loyalty-club style labels that match neither the spec's tier ladder (Iron → Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum → Diamond → Olympian) nor anything the backend awarded. A level-4 user is proclaimed 'GOLD MEMBER' purely by a client-side guess. Also low contrast: the pale-gold chip (#FFD183 on 12% gold tint over the light page) is far below WCAG text contrast.
findings.fix: Show nothing (or the numeric level) when levelName is missing, and use spec tier names with a readable color if a fallback must exist.

findings.title: Settings 'Export my data' card ends with a stray trailing divider
findings.evidence: _Tile(icon: Icons.download_outlined, title: 'Export my data', ... onTap: _exportData,), const _Divider(),  ]),
findings.detail: The card contains a single tile followed by a divider with nothing after it, leaving a floating hairline above the card's bottom edge — looks like a row failed to render (likely a leftover from when the old inline delete-account tile lived below it, per the comment at line 619).
findings.fix: Remove the trailing const _Divider().

findings.title: Change-password dialog: silent dead-end when token is null, and no 128-char max enforced
findings.evidence: final token = await _auth.getAccessToken(); if (token == null) return; — Update button returns with the dialog still open and no message
findings.detail: If the session can't produce a token, tapping Update does literally nothing — no error, dialog stays. Additionally only the 8-char minimum is validated (line 482); the spec's 8-128 range upper bound is unchecked, deferring a confusing server error.
findings.fix: Show 'Session expired — sign in again' on null token, and add a 128-char max check next to the existing min check.

findings.title: Settings privacy toggles: cold-start race between cached prefs and server values
findings.evidence: await Future.wait([_loadProfile(), _loadIntegrations(), ... _loadPrefs(), ...]); — _loadPrefs (reads SharedPreferences cache) and _loadProfile (reads server then overwrites the same cache) run concurrently with no ordering
findings.detail: Both loaders setState the same three toggle fields. In the common case prefs resolve before HTTP so server wins, but nothing guarantees it — if _loadPrefs lands after _loadProfile, a stale cached value silently overwrites the fresh server truth until the next visit. Low practical impact, but it is the kind of toggle 'flicker back' bug that is impossible to reproduce on demand.
findings.fix: Run _loadPrefs first (sequentially) for instant render, then let _loadProfile override when it returns.

findings.title: Feed filter pills (All / Following / Popular) never reload the feed
findings.evidence: onTap: () { if (f.id == 'races') { _openRaceHub(); return; } setState(() => _feedFilter = f.id); }
findings.detail: Tapping a filter pill only mutates _feedFilter and repaints the pill highlight — it never calls _onFilterChanged()/_load(), so the feed content does not change. Compare the trending/friends toggle at line 887 which correctly calls _onFilterChanged(). The posts list is server-filtered only (build() does no local filtering on _feedFilter), so 'Following' and 'Popular' are visually selected but functionally inert until something else (pull-to-refresh, push hint) happens to reload. The empty-state 'Reset filter' button (line 472-476) does call _onFilterChanged(), confirming the intended pattern.
findings.fix: Replace `setState(() => _feedFilter = f.id);` with `_feedFilter = f.id; _onFilterChanged();`. Also note that with the default _trendingFilter='trending', _filterParams() overwrites sort='popular' with 'trending' (line 104-105), so the Popular pill would still be shadowed — give 'popular' precedence or clear the toggle when Popular is selected.

findings.title: Hide / Block / Delete on the main feed leave the post card visible (onDelete not wired)
findings.evidence: (_, i) => SocialFeedPostCard(key: ValueKey(_posts[i].id), post: _posts[i], service: _feedService, onLike: () {},)
findings.detail: SocialFeedPostCard's 'Hide this post', 'Block user' and 'Delete post' actions all finish with `widget.onDelete?.call()` (social_feed_post_card.dart lines 160, 263, 361) to let the parent remove the card. The main feed never passes onDelete (or onEdit), so after a successful hide/block/delete the post stays on screen until the next full reload. For Block this is the worst case: the user gets a '$name blocked' snackbar while the blocked user's post remains in front of them. Edited captions are similarly not reflected (onEdit null). PostDetailScreen wires both correctly (post_detail_screen.dart lines 392-393), proving the contract.
findings.fix: In the feed's SliverChildBuilderDelegate pass `onDelete: () => setState(() => _posts.removeAt(i))` (or remove by id) and `onEdit: (updated) => setState(() => _posts[i] = updated)`.

findings.title: Like state never initialized from server — already-liked posts show an empty heart, and tapping it silently unlikes
findings.evidence: bool _liked = false;  // and SocialFeedPost.fromJson parses no liked/likedByMe field (social_feed_post.dart:91-135)
findings.detail: Every card starts with _liked=false regardless of whether the current user already liked the post. SocialFeedPost has likeCount/commentCount but no viewer-liked flag, and fromJson never attempts to read one. Consequence: after any feed reload all hearts render unliked; a user who taps the heart on a post they previously liked optimistically sees +1, then the server toggle returns liked=false and the reconcile path (lines 394-399) drops the count by 2 and empties the heart — the exact opposite of the user's intent. Like state is effectively not persisted across sessions/reloads.
findings.fix: Add `likedByMe` to SocialFeedPost (parse e.g. j['likedByMe'] / j['viewerLiked']), seed `_liked = widget.post.likedByMe` in initState, and ask backend to include it in GET /v1/posts/feed if it doesn't already.

findings.title: getComments / addComment / getPost have no timeout and no network-error handling — comments sheet can spin forever and the send button can lock up
findings.evidence: final res = await http.get(Uri.parse('$v1Base/posts/$postId/comments'), headers: headers);  // no .timeout, no SocketException catch (same for addComment:288 and getPost:210)
findings.detail: Unlike getFeedPage/toggleLike which wrap SocketException/TimeoutException, these three calls throw raw network exceptions. Downstream: SocialCommentsSheet._load (social_feed_post_card.dart:831-835) has no try/catch, so on a network error `_loading` stays true and the sheet shows an infinite spinner with no retry. SocialCommentsSheet._send (line 845) and PostDetailScreen._sendComment (post_detail_screen.dart:104) await addComment unprotected — an exception leaves `_sending=true` permanently, disabling the send button for the rest of the sheet's life with no feedback. This also breaks the offline-first expectation: airplane mode + open comments = hang, not an honest error state.
findings.fix: Apply the same try/SocketException/TimeoutException → SocialFeedException pattern plus .timeout(AuthService.httpTimeout) to getComments, addComment and getPost; wrap the UI awaits in try/catch that clears _loading/_sending and shows an error state with retry.

findings.title: Gallery load failure renders the 'No posts yet — Be the first to share your progress!' empty state instead of an error
findings.evidence: catch (e, st) { reportError(...); if (mounted) { setState(() { _loading = false; _hasMore = false; }); } }  → line 289: if (!_loading && _posts.isEmpty) return const ZveltEmptyState(... title: 'No posts yet', subtitle: 'Be the first to share your progress!');
findings.detail: Both the non-200 branch (lines 110-116) and the catch branch swallow the failure and fall through to the empty state. A user offline or hitting a 500 is told the community has zero posts — a dishonest empty state that directly violates the 'real data or honest empty/connect state' principle. There is also no pull-to-refresh and no retry affordance, so the only recovery is leaving and reopening the screen.
findings.fix: Track an _error field like BookmarksScreen does and render ZveltErrorState with onRetry; reserve the 'No posts yet' copy for a successful 200 with an empty list. Add RefreshIndicator.

findings.title: Stories have zero moderation: no report, no block filtering, no way to delete your own story
findings.evidence: loadActiveStories() never consults ModerationService.getCachedBlockedIds() (contrast social_feed_service.dart:118-121), the stories table stores 'user_id': null (line 390), and `deleteStory` (line 463) is never called from any UI (grep: only the definition exists).
findings.detail: Stories are other-user UGC fetched from /v1/stories/feed, yet: (1) the _StoryViewerScreen offers only a Close button — no report/block surface, while posts and comments both have them (the codebase itself cites Apple §1.2 as the reason those exist); (2) blocked users' stories keep appearing in the stories row because the blocked-IDs defense-in-depth filter applied to feed and comments is absent here, and the local rows don't even persist a user_id to filter on; (3) a user who posts a story cannot remove it — deleteStory is fully implemented but unreachable, so an accidental post stays up for 24h.
