findings.fix: Store userId in the stories table and filter by getCachedBlockedIds() in loadActiveStories; add a long-press/overflow menu in _StoryViewerScreen with 'Delete' (own stories, wiring deleteStory) and 'Report user' / 'Block' (others').

findings.title: Bookmark toggle reports failure as 'unbookmarked', silently removing posts from the Bookmarks screen on server errors
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode != 200 && res.statusCode != 201) { return false; }
findings.detail: toggleBookmark conflates 'server says not bookmarked' with 'request failed'. In BookmarksScreen every card has initiallyBookmarked=true; if the user taps the bookmark icon and the server returns 500, toggleBookmark returns false, the card calls onBookmarkChanged(false), and bookmarks_screen.dart:118-131 animates the row away — even though the post is still bookmarked server-side. In the feed the same false return shows the icon as un-saved after a failed save with no error message. The network-exception path in the card's bottom button (social_feed_post_card.dart:755-758) also reverts silently with no snackbar, unlike every other action.
findings.fix: Make toggleBookmark throw SocialFeedException on non-2xx (and catch SocketException/Timeout like its siblings); in the card, show a snackbar on failure and do not invoke onBookmarkChanged.

findings.title: Notification timestamps render as 'just now ago' / '5m ago ago'
findings.evidence: Text('$timeAgo ago', ...)  where timeAgo = safeRelativeTime(n.createdAt) and relative_time.dart:26-28 already returns 'just now' / '${diff.inMinutes}m ago'
findings.detail: safeRelativeTime already appends 'ago' (or returns 'just now', or a date like 'Mar 14'). The card template adds a second ' ago', producing 'just now ago', '2h ago ago', and 'Mar 14 ago' for week-old items. Visible on every row of the Activity screen.
findings.fix: Change to Text(timeAgo, ...).

findings.title: All notifications sit under a hardcoded 'EARLIER TODAY' section header regardless of age
findings.evidence: _buildSectionLabel('EARLIER TODAY'),  // single static label above the whole _items list
findings.detail: Week-old likes and friend requests (the page size is 40 and paginates further back) are labeled 'EARLIER TODAY'. The label even shows above the 'No notifications yet' empty state. This mislabels real data — a smaller cousin of the fabricated-data problem the owner cares about.
findings.fix: Either group items into Today / This week / Earlier buckets from n.createdAt, or drop the section label entirely.

findings.title: Notifications API failure is shown as 'No notifications yet' (silent empty page on non-200)
findings.evidence: if (res.statusCode != 200) { return const NotificationsPage(items: [], hasMore: false); }
findings.detail: listPage swallows 401/500 into an empty page, so NotificationsScreen's error path (which exists and renders ZveltErrorState) is unreachable for HTTP errors — the user sees the 'No notifications yet' empty state instead. The calls also have no .timeout, so a hung connection leaves the initial spinner indefinitely.
findings.fix: Throw a typed exception on non-200 (mirroring SocialFeedException) and add .timeout; let the screen's existing _error/ZveltErrorState handle it.

findings.title: Gallery filter taps are silently dropped while a page fetch is in flight
findings.evidence: Future<void> _loadPosts({bool reset = false}) async { if (_fetchingMore) return;  ...  // pill onTap (231-233): setState(() => _filter = f); _loadPosts(reset: true);
findings.detail: If the user taps RECENT → POPULAR while a pagination request is running, _filter updates (pill highlights) but _loadPosts(reset:true) early-returns. The grid keeps showing the previous filter's posts with a stale _page counter, and nothing ever retries. There is also no generation guard, so a slow first-filter response can land after the filter changed.
findings.fix: Use a generation counter like SocialPlusScreen._loadGen: bump it on filter change, let reset loads proceed (cancelling/ignoring the in-flight page), and discard stale responses.

findings.title: Always-on green presence dot on the Circle button fabricates activity
findings.evidence: Positioned(top: -2, right: -2, child: Container(width: 10, height: 10, decoration: BoxDecoration(shape: BoxShape.circle, color: ZveltTokens.success, ...)))
findings.detail: The green badge on the Circle header button is rendered unconditionally — it is not bound to unread requests, online friends, or any data. It permanently signals 'something new in your circle' even for a brand-new account with zero friends. This is a fabricated indicator, the exact pattern the product principles prohibit.
findings.fix: Bind the dot to a real signal (e.g. pending friend-request count from the circle scope, or SocialNotificationHub.unreadCount) and hide it when zero.

findings.title: 'RACE OF THE WEEK' / 'TRENDING RACE' labels are applied to merely the newest challenge
findings.evidence: /// TODO(v1.1): swap to a `trendingScore` field ... SocialChallenge? get _topChallenge => _challenges.isEmpty ? null : _challenges.first;  // and line 724: label: 'TRENDING RACE'
findings.detail: The hero card crowns whatever challenge was created most recently as 'RACE OF THE WEEK' and pins a hardcoded 'TRENDING RACE' badge on it, even if it has zero participants (the subtitle can simultaneously say 'Be the first to join'). The TODO acknowledges this. Until a real trending signal exists, the copy asserts popularity that is not in the data.
findings.fix: Until trendingScore ships, use honest copy: 'NEWEST RACE' / 'LATEST RACE', and drop the 'TRENDING RACE' badge or gate it on participant count.

findings.title: Story posted offline is never synced later — it silently stays local-only
findings.evidence: // Best-effort server post — server still expects base64 in the body. ... } catch (e, st) { reportError(e, st, reason: 'stories:add-post'); }  // no retry, and imageBase64 is not persisted for later upload
findings.detail: addStory inserts locally first (good, offline-first) then POSTs once, best-effort. If the POST fails (offline, timeout), the failure is only logged; the base64 payload is discarded (the DB row stores only a file path) and no outbox/retry exists — unlike reports, which got ReportOutboxService for exactly this case. The user sees their story in the row for 24h and reasonably believes friends can see it; they never can. The composer also shows no distinction between synced and local-only stories.
findings.fix: Add a `synced` flag to the stories row; on loadActiveStories (or app foreground), re-POST unsynced rows by re-reading bytes from image_file_path. Optionally mark unsynced bubbles with a small 'not posted yet' indicator.

findings.title: Pull-to-refresh on the feed replaces the whole list with a full-screen spinner and loses scroll position
findings.evidence: setState(() { _loading = true; ... });  // and build() line 433: _loading ? const Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator(...)) : ... RefreshIndicator(onRefresh: _load, ...)
findings.detail: _load is shared by initial load, pull-to-refresh, and FeedRefreshNotifier hints. Because it always sets _loading=true, any refresh — including a background push hint arriving while the user is reading mid-feed — tears down the CustomScrollView, shows a centered spinner, and rebuilds at scroll offset 0. The RefreshIndicator's own spinner is immediately replaced by the full-screen one.
findings.fix: Only set _loading=true when _posts is empty; otherwise refresh in place (keep showing current posts until the new page arrives).

findings.title: Sub-44pt touch targets on '+ POST', '+ NEW', filter pills, and the trending toggle
findings.evidence: GestureDetector(onTap: _newPost, child: Container(height: 28, ...child: const Text('+ POST', ...)))  // '+ NEW' is height: 26 (line 995), filter pills height: 30 (line 943), toggle height: 34 (line 874)
findings.detail: The primary post-creation CTA is a 28px-tall bare GestureDetector; '+ NEW' (challenge creation) is 26px; the feed filter pills are 30px; the trending toggle is 34px. All are below the 44pt minimum the project mandates, and none carry Semantics(button:) labels, unlike the post-card actions which were carefully done. Gallery's filter pills (gallery_screen.dart:236) have the same 30px issue.
findings.fix: Wrap each in a SizedBox(height: 44)+Center or add vertical hit padding via behavior+padding; add Semantics(button: true, label: ...).

findings.title: Gallery grid tiles are not tappable — posts cannot be opened from Explore
findings.evidence: return ClipRRect(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4), child: Stack(fit: StackFit.expand, children: [ ... ]));  // no GestureDetector/InkWell anywhere in itemBuilder
findings.detail: Each grid tile has an id (normalised at line 141) and PostDetailScreen exists, but tiles have no tap handler. Users can see thumbnails and like counts but cannot open a post, read its caption, like, or comment — a dead end on a screen named 'EXPLORE MOMENTUM'.
findings.fix: Wrap the tile in GestureDetector → Navigator.push(PostDetailScreen(postId: post['id'])).

findings.title: Off-token colors and fonts: legacy orange gradient, gray #6B7280, pink #EC4899, SpaceGrotesk
findings.evidence: colors: [Color(0xFFFF5A1F), Color(0xFFD9360B)]  // tokens define brand=#FF7A2F, brandDeep=#E86B24 and ZveltTokens.gradBtn
findings.detail: The social surface repeatedly paints a different orange than the brand: the FF5A1F→D9360B gradient appears on the Circle button (573), trending toggle (896), and '+ POST' (1073), and again in gallery_screen.dart:32 — all visibly hotter/darker than ZveltTokens.gradBtn. Hardcoded Color(0xFF6B7280) is used for secondary text in 5+ places (social_plus 905, 987, 1061; notifications 308, 332, 473) where text2/text3 exist. notifications_screen.dart:222 uses fontFamily 'SpaceGrotesk' while tokens define only Inter/IBMPlexMono, and :377 hardcodes pink 0xFFEC4899. The post card's hero stat block hardcodes 0xFFFFE0C0/0xFFFFD3AA/0xFF0A0A0C (social_feed_post_card.dart:558-592) instead of surfaceTinted/brandTint/text.
findings.fix: Sweep the area for Color(0x...) literals and map them to ZveltTokens (gradBtn, text2/text3, surfaceTinted); standardize headers on fontPrimary.

findings.title: Story viewer crashes (latent) if constructed with an empty list — clamp runs before the isEmpty guard
findings.evidence: _index = widget.initialIndex.clamp(0, widget.stories.length - 1);  // line 1463's `if (widget.stories.isEmpty)` guard runs after this
findings.detail: For an empty list, clamp(0, -1) throws ArgumentError (lower > upper) before the empty-list early-pop at line 1463 ever executes, so that defensive branch is unreachable dead protection. Currently safe only because _viewStory (line 314) guards the call site.
findings.fix: Move the isEmpty check above the clamp, or use `widget.stories.isEmpty ? 0 : initialIndex.clamp(0, stories.length - 1)`.

findings.title: Feed card comment count goes stale after commenting; onLike callback is a no-op in feed and bookmarks
findings.evidence: value: '${post.commentCount} comments',  // SocialCommentsSheet reloads internally but never notifies the card; feed passes onLike: () {} (social_plus_screen.dart:495)
findings.detail: Adding a comment via the sheet updates the sheet's list but the card under it keeps the old count until a full feed reload. The required onLike parameter is an empty closure in both feed and bookmarks, so it exists only for PostDetailScreen — a mild API smell that hides the missing count-sync.
findings.fix: Have SocialCommentsSheet return the new count on close (Navigator.pop(count)) and update a local _commentCount in the card, mirroring _likeCount.

findings.title: Comment composers have no 500-char limit (spec mandates max 500 for comment.body)
findings.evidence: TextField(controller: _ctrl, decoration: const InputDecoration(hintText: 'Add a comment...'), onSubmitted: (_) => _send(),)  // no maxLength; same in post_detail_screen.dart:507
findings.detail: CLAUDE.md validation table caps comment.body at 500 chars. Both comment inputs accept unlimited text; an over-limit comment fails server-side and surfaces only as the generic 'Could not post comment' snackbar (addComment returns bool with no message), so the user gets no hint why.
findings.fix: Add maxLength: 500 (and maxLines) to both comment TextFields, matching the edit-caption dialog which already does this.

findings.title: Story composer _save has no error handling — a thrown addStory leaves the sheet stuck on the saving spinner
findings.evidence: await _service.addStory(story); if (!mounted) return; Navigator.of(context).pop(true);  // no try/catch; addStory can `throw StateError('Story image too large ...')` (social_story_service.dart:358)
findings.detail: addStory swallows most failures internally but explicitly throws on >5MB images. If it throws, _saving stays true (button disabled forever) and the sheet never closes. Low likelihood today because the picker caps at 1280px/q75, but it is an unguarded await on a throwing API in UI code.
findings.fix: Wrap in try/catch, reset _saving and show a snackbar on failure.

findings.title: Tapping any notification reloads page 1 and discards pagination/scroll position
findings.evidence: await _load();  // unconditional at the end of _onTap, after returning from the pushed screen
findings.detail: A user 3 pages deep who opens one notification returns to a reset list at the top (_load clears _items and _page=1). Marking one item read only needs a local state flip plus the unread-badge refresh.
findings.fix: Update the tapped item's readAt locally (copyWith) and call SocialNotificationHub.refresh() instead of a full _load().

findings.title: Push token is never unregistered from backend on logout (token wiped before teardown)
findings.evidence: stopOnLogout(): `final jwt = await _auth.getAccessToken(); if (token != null && jwt != null) { await http.delete(...'/me/push-token'...)` — but main.dart:465 runs `await _auth.logout();` (which wipes tokens) BEFORE main.dart:482 calls `PushMessagingService.instance.stopOnLogout()`.
findings.detail: Because the auth tokens are cleared first (intentionally, per the 'local-first sign-out' comment at main.dart:460), jwt is always null when stopOnLogout() runs, so the DELETE /v1/me/push-token branch is unreachable in the real logout flow — the server-side registration is never cleaned up on any logout. Impact is mitigated by FirebaseMessaging.deleteToken() (the stale token becomes undeliverable), but the backend accumulates dead registrations tied to the logged-out account and the unregister code is effectively dead.
findings.fix: Capture the JWT (or call stopOnLogout) before AuthService.logout() wipes tokens — e.g. fetch the access token into a local variable first, or have stopOnLogout accept a pre-fetched token.

findings.title: Notification tap handler can be silently clobbered — three services initialize flutter_local_notifications, only one registers the tap callback
findings.evidence: PlannedWorkoutReminderService.ensureInitialized() calls `await _plugin.initialize(settings: ...)` with NO onDidReceiveNotificationResponse; push_messaging_service.dart:153 is the only initialize that registers `onDidReceiveNotificationResponse: (response) { _onLocalNotificationTap(response.payload); }`; retention_reminder_service.dart:27 also initializes without a callback.
findings.detail: FlutterLocalNotificationsPlugin wraps a singleton platform implementation, so the last initialize() call wins for the tap callback. Realistic sequence: foreground DM arrives (push service initializes and sets the deep-link callback) → user toggles a planned workout in the calendar (activity_calendar_screen.dart:514 triggers PlannedWorkoutReminderService.ensureInitialized, re-initializing with a null callback) → user taps the DM banner → nothing happens. Each service also keeps its own `_initialized` flag, so they cannot detect each other.
findings.fix: Centralize a single FlutterLocalNotificationsPlugin initialization (one shared service) that always registers the payload-routing callback, and have retention/planned/push services share it.

findings.title: Local notification taps after process death are dropped — getNotificationAppLaunchDetails is never checked
findings.evidence: `_onLocalNotificationTap(String? payload)` is only reachable via the live onDidReceiveNotificationResponse callback; no caller of `getNotificationAppLaunchDetails()` exists anywhere in lib/ (grep finds none). Planned reminders even carry `payload: e.id` (planned_workout_reminder_service.dart:86) that nothing ever reads.
findings.detail: If the user receives a foreground DM banner, kills the app, and later taps the banner from the tray, the app cold-starts but the conversation deep-link payload is discarded. Planned-workout reminder taps are a dead end in all states since their plugin instance registers no callback at all — the payload (plan id) is write-only.
findings.fix: On bootstrap, call getNotificationAppLaunchDetails() and route `notificationResponse.payload` through navigateFromPushData / a plan-detail route; register a tap callback for planned reminders.

findings.title: Tapping a DM push while already inside that conversation stacks a duplicate DirectChatScreen
findings.evidence: case 'dm_message': `nav.push(MaterialPageRoute<void>(builder: (_) => DirectChatScreen(conversationId: cid, ...)))` — always pushes; there is no active-conversation check anywhere (grep for activeConversation/suppress finds nothing), and _onForegroundFcmMessage (line 192-201) shows the Android banner even when that chat is currently open.
findings.detail: While chatting with someone, every incoming message from them raises a banner for the chat you are looking at; tapping it pushes a second DirectChatScreen on top of the first (and a third, etc.). Back navigation then walks through duplicate chat screens.
findings.fix: Track the currently-open conversationId (e.g. static field set by DirectChatScreen initState/dispose); skip the banner when it matches, and in navigateFromPushData use pushReplacement/popUntil or skip the push when that conversation is already topmost.

findings.title: scheduleStreakAtRiskOnce is dead code — the 'streak at risk' notification never fires
findings.evidence: `Future<void> scheduleStreakAtRiskOnce({required int streak})` has zero callers (project-wide grep matches only the definition). Meanwhile skeleton_home_tab.dart:231 computes `_streakAtRisk = streak?['isAtRisk'] as bool? ?? false;` for the in-app banner only.
findings.detail: The spec's streak-at-risk alert exists as fully-written code but is never invoked, so users only learn their streak is at risk if they happen to open the app. Two latent bugs if it ever gets wired: (1) line 134 stamps `_prefsStreakAtRiskDate` BEFORE scheduling, so a scheduling failure suppresses retries for the rest of the day; (2) line 157 uses `AndroidScheduleMode.exactAllowWhileIdle`, which throws PlatformException on Android 13/14 when the exact-alarm permission is not granted, and the call is not wrapped in try/catch.
findings.fix: Wire it to the streak check in skeleton_home_tab (call when `_streakAtRisk` is true), move the prefs stamp after a successful zonedSchedule, switch to inexactAllowWhileIdle (8 PM does not need exactness), and wrap in try/catch.

findings.title: Reminder toggle reports success on iOS even when notification permission was denied
findings.evidence: `final allowedAndroid = await androidImpl?.areNotificationsEnabled(); if (allowedAndroid == false) { ... return false; }` — only the Android result is checked; the iOS `iosImpl.requestPermissions(...)` return value (line 73) is ignored.
findings.detail: On iOS, if the user denies the permission dialog, setEnabled still persists `_prefsEnabled = true` and returns true, so the home-tab toggle (skeleton_home_tab.dart:576-582) shows the reminder as active and never shows the 'Enable notifications in system settings' snackbar — but no notification will ever be delivered. The UI state lies about a feature that silently does nothing.
findings.fix: Capture the bool returned by `iosImpl.requestPermissions(...)` and treat false the same as `allowedAndroid == false` (persist false, return false).

findings.title: Notifications list shows the empty state on server errors (5xx rendered as 'no notifications')
findings.evidence: listPage(): `if (res.statusCode != 200) { return const NotificationsPage(items: [], hasMore: false); }` — notifications_screen.dart:65 awaits this and only its catch block (network exceptions) sets `_error`.
findings.detail: When the backend returns 401/500, the screen renders the empty state instead of the error state, telling the user they have no notifications when the truth is 'we could not load them'. This conflicts with the no-dishonest-states principle and with the hardened markAllRead() in the same file (lines 124-142), which was explicitly fixed to throw on non-2xx for exactly this reason. Same pattern in unreadCount() (line 78: non-200 → 0).
findings.fix: Throw NotificationsException on non-200 in listPage (mirroring markAllRead) so notifications_screen's existing _error path renders ZveltErrorState; also during pagination, distinguish error from end-of-list.

findings.title: Dead race-chat store contains fabricated users and a fabricated message attributed to the user
findings.evidence: seedFor('global') returns hardcoded fake people and a fake first-person message: `const RaceChatMessage(isMe: true, time: '09:45 AM', text: '40? Ăla e doar încălzirea mea, Marco. ...')` plus 'Marco.K' / 'Elena_S' messages and a fake 'Alex_Fit joined the race' system line.
findings.detail: RaceChatStore has zero callers (grep: only self-references) — the live implementation is race_chat_service.dart (SQLCipher, self-only echo, honest about it in its header). But this file directly violates principle #1: it seeds invented chat from invented people, including words put in the USER'S mouth (isMe: true), and its Romanian copy clashes with the English UI. If anyone ever revives this store, the app fabricates a social scene.
findings.fix: Delete lib/services/race_chat_store.dart (and its prefs key migration if any persisted seeds exist under 'zvelt_race_chat_v1').

findings.title: Mixed Romanian/English user-facing copy in an English-language app
findings.evidence: app_strings.dart declares 'App copy in English' and all strings are English, but live screens hardcode Romanian UI text: health_screen.dart:170 `return 'Actualizare necesară';`, health_screen.dart:221 `label: 'Reîncearcă'`, segment_leaderboard_screen.dart:495 `label: 'Distanță'` and :511 `label: 'Recordul tău'`, heatmap_screen.dart:686 `'Nicio activitate înregistrată'`, auth_service.dart:14 `'Nu mă pot conecta la server ($apiBaseUrl)...'` (shown to users on connection failure).
findings.detail: The l10n layer (app_strings.dart, auth_error_messages.dart which translates backend RO→EN) establishes English as the product language, yet at least four user-visible surfaces (Health Connect screen, segment leaderboard labels, heatmap empty state, the auth connection error) ship Romanian. A user hitting the heatmap empty state or a dead backend sees a different language than the rest of the app.
findings.fix: Move these strings into AppStrings in English; the auth_service connection-error string also leaks dev instructions ('Pornește backend-ul pe PC') that should never reach production users.

findings.title: StartCountdown renders a white 200px digit on the near-white V2 background (leftover dark-theme styling)
findings.evidence: Digit style: `color: Colors.white` with only `Shadow(color: widget.accent.withValues(alpha: 0.7), blurRadius: 60)` for separation; the screen background (line 72) is `colors: [widget.accent.withValues(alpha: 0.25), ZveltTokens.bg]` where ZveltTokens.bg = Color(0xFFF6F7F5) (zvelt_tokens.dart:21).
findings.detail: After the V2 light-theme migration (the exact class of bug documented in zvelt_primary_button.dart:73-84), this widget still assumes a dark backdrop: white-on-#F6F7F5 is ~1.05:1 contrast — the 3·2·1 digit is legible only through its colored glow. Live in the outdoor run/bike start flow (outdoor_track_screen.dart:137). The 'Cancel' label (line 126, text2 at 0.7 alpha) is also borderline on the light gradient.
findings.fix: Use ZveltTokens.text (or the accent color at full opacity) for the digit, and full-opacity text2 for Cancel; alternatively keep white but darken the overlay gradient.

findings.title: AI coach chat: conversation is not persisted and there is no welcome/empty state
findings.evidence: `final List<_Msg> _msgs = [];` — plain in-memory state on a StatefulWidget; nothing in the file (or AiChatService) loads or saves history. On first open the ListView renders zero items with only the small disclaimer paragraph above it.
findings.detail: Leaving the screen (including the automatic push to WorkoutTrackerScreen at line 96 after the AI creates a workout — when the user finishes and pops back to a fresh navigation later, or backs out of chat) discards the whole coaching conversation, including the 7-day micro plan and risks the AI just produced. There is also no empty state inviting a first question — the screen opens to a blank list, which reads as broken next to the polished ZveltEmptyState used elsewhere.
findings.fix: Persist messages locally (SharedPreferences/sqflite, like race_chat_service does) and reload on open; add a compact ZveltEmptyState ('Ask your coach anything about training or nutrition') when _msgs is empty and not busy.

findings.title: Icon-only controls lack semantic labels (AI send button, nav quick-action FAB)
findings.evidence: `IconButton.filled(onPressed: _busy ? null : _send, icon: const Icon(Icons.send), ...)` — no tooltip/semantic label; same for the nav-bar quick FAB (zvelt_main_nav_bar.dart:213-237: InkWell + bare `Icon(Icons.add_rounded)`, no Semantics/tooltip) and the nav cells (zvelt_main_nav_bar.dart:100) which never expose `Semantics(selected: ...)`.
findings.detail: Screen readers announce these as unlabeled buttons. The send button is the primary action of the AI chat; the center FAB is the app's main quick-action entry point. Tab cells announce their text label but not the selected state.
findings.fix: Add `tooltip: 'Send'` to the IconButton, wrap the FAB in `Semantics(label: 'Quick actions', button: true)`, and wrap nav cells in `Semantics(selected: selected, button: true)`.

findings.title: ScrambleText with empty text spins a 36ms setState loop forever
findings.evidence: _onTick(): `if (len == 0) { setState(() {}); return; }` — the periodic Timer (line 78, default tick 36ms) is never cancelled for empty text, so an empty-string ScrambleText rebuilds ~28 times/second until disposed.
findings.detail: Any caller that passes a not-yet-loaded string ('' while data fetches) burns CPU/battery on continuous no-op rebuilds. The non-empty completion path correctly cancels (line 105).
findings.fix: Cancel the timer when len == 0 (and restart from didUpdateWidget when text becomes non-empty, which already happens).

findings.title: markRead is fire-and-forget with no timeout or status check
findings.evidence: `Future<void> markRead(String id) async { await http.post(Uri.parse('$v1Base/notifications/$id/read'), headers: await _headers()); }` — no .timeout, no statusCode check, contrasting with markAllRead in the same file which was hardened (12s timeout + typed exception) specifically because 'UI would optimistically mark items read locally even when the server 500'd'.
findings.detail: The single-item path has exactly the drift bug the markAllRead comment (lines 114-123) describes as fixed: a failed POST leaves the item read locally but unread server-side, and a hung connection awaits indefinitely.
findings.fix: Apply the same pattern as markAllRead: 12s timeout, throw NotificationsException on non-2xx, let the row revert its optimistic state on failure.

findings.title: AI chat user bubble: white 15px text on brand orange fails WCAG AA contrast
findings.evidence: `color: user ? ZveltTokens.brand : ZveltTokens.surface` with text `color: user ? ZveltTokens.onBrand : ZveltTokens.text, fontSize: 15` — onBrand is #FFFFFF on brand #FF7A2F (zvelt_tokens.dart:51,61), ≈2.5:1 contrast vs the 4.5:1 AA requirement for 15px body text.
findings.detail: This is a token-level pairing (onBrand-on-brand) so it appears wherever body-size text sits on the brand fill; in this area it makes every user message in the AI chat low-contrast. Large/bold CTA labels mostly squeak by as 'large text', but 15px regular chat copy does not.
findings.fix: For body-size text on brand, darken the bubble (e.g. brandDeep) or use dark text; alternatively reserve brand fill for >=18px bold and use surface+border bubbles with brand accents for user messages.

findings.title: Re-enabling a past-dated planned workout schedules a 'Today: …' notification 2 minutes from now
findings.evidence: _scheduleTime(): `if (!at.isAfter(now)) at = now.add(const Duration(minutes: 2));` then the notification body is `'Today: ${e.title}. Get ready.'` (line 85).
findings.detail: Un-completing a plan dated yesterday (or any past date) via the calendar (activity_calendar_screen.dart:514) fires a 'Today: X. Get ready.' notification two minutes later for a workout that is not today's — a small but surprising, dishonest nudge. Same happens for today's plans toggled after 9 AM, where it is more defensible.
findings.fix: Skip scheduling entirely when the plan date is before today instead of falling back to now+2min; only apply the fallback for today's entries.
